You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_68.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 68: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[68]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_68.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 68: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""ReAct analysis agent — autonomous tool selection for evidence analysis.
2	
3	Supports three pipeline modes (PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE env var):
4	- "8phase" (default): 6-phase adaptive pipeline when PG_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD=1:
5	    Plan→Execute→Briefing+Classify→Scaffold(5-lens)→GapFill→Write+SelfRefine→Verify
6	  Falls back to legacy 8-phase (critique→rewrite) when PG_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD=0.
7	- "legacy": Plan→Execute→Interpret→Verify (2 LLM calls, ReWOO pattern)
8	- "react": Per-step LLM decisions (up to 5+1 LLM calls)
9	
10	The adaptive pipeline fixes 6 loopholes identified by red team review:
11	1. Time budget trap → deleted critique+rewrite, 75s headroom
12	2. Refinement spaghetti → SELF-REFINE absorbs critique+rewrite
13	3. Length guard kills artifacts → table-aware bypass
14	4. Semantic vector trap → JSON gap queries with positive phrasing
15	5. Self-scoring sycophancy → boolean checklist, not 1-10 score
16	6. Intent anchoring bias → WILL not WON'T in intent brief
17	
18	The agent enforces citation provenance: every analysis result traces back
19	to original evidence IDs, never "POLARIS Analysis Toolkit".
20	"""
21	
22	import asyncio
23	import json
24	import logging
25	import os
26	import re
27	import time
28	
29	import numpy as np
30	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator, model_validator
31	from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import fcluster, linkage
32	from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist
33	
34	from src.polaris_graph.tools.analysis_notebook import AnalysisNotebook, AnalysisStep
35	from src.polaris_graph.tools.analysis_toolkit import _safe_float
36	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import (
37	    ToolRegistry,
38	    ToolResult,
39	    build_default_registry,
40	)
41	from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_text, embed_texts
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
44	
45	_MAX_ITERATIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_MAX_ITERATIONS", "5"))
46	_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "900"))
47	_TOOL_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_TOOL_TIMEOUT", "60"))
48	_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT", "240"))
49	
50	# 8-phase pipeline env vars
51	_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE = os.getenv("PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE", "8phase")
52	_DOMAIN_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD = float(
53	    os.getenv("PG_DOMAIN_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD", "0.15"),
54	)
55	_LEARNINGS_PER_CLUSTER = int(os.getenv("PG_LEARNINGS_PER_CLUSTER", "8"))
56	_EVIDENCE_PER_CLUSTER = int(os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_PER_CLUSTER", "5"))
57	_MAX_CLUSTERS = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_CLUSTERS", "15"))
58	_MAX_GAP_EVIDENCE = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_GAP_EVIDENCE", "15"))
59	_MAX_INTERPRETATION_REWRITES = int(
60	    os.getenv("PG_MAX_INTERPRETATION_REWRITES", "1"),
61	)
62	_SCAFFOLD_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_SCAFFOLD_TIMEOUT", "240"))
63	_CRITIQUE_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_CRITIQUE_TIMEOUT", "180"))
64	
65	# 6-phase adaptive pipeline env vars
66	_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD = os.getenv("PG_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD", "1") == "1"
67	_REFINER_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_REFINER_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
68	_SELF_REFINE_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_SELF_REFINE_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
69	_SELF_REFINE_MAX_ITERATIONS = int(
70	    os.getenv("PG_SELF_REFINE_MAX_ITERATIONS", "2"),
71	)
72	
73	# WS-5: CiteFix post-processing (ACL 2025 Industry)
74	_CITEFIX_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_CITEFIX_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
75	_CITEFIX_KEYWORD_MIN = int(os.getenv("PG_CITEFIX_KEYWORD_MIN", "3"))
76	_CITEFIX_SEMANTIC_THRESHOLD = float(
77	    os.getenv("PG_CITEFIX_SEMANTIC_THRESHOLD", "0.50"),
78	)
79	
80	# Generate-Then-Attribute: separate prose generation from citation placement
81	# When ON, scaffold uses (refs: ev_xxx) metadata instead of [CITE:ev_xxx],
82	# write prompts produce citation-free prose, and _attribute_citations()
83	# adds citations programmatically at sentence boundaries using 3 strategies:
84	# number matching → keyword overlap → embedding similarity.
85	_GTA_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_GENERATE_THEN_ATTRIBUTE", "0") == "1"
86	_GTA_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_ATTRIBUTION_THRESHOLD", "0.35"))
87	_GTA_MAX_PER_SENTENCE = int(
88	    os.getenv("PG_ATTRIBUTION_MAX_PER_SENTENCE", "3"),
89	)
90	_GTA_KEYWORD_MIN = int(os.getenv("PG_ATTRIBUTION_KEYWORD_MIN", "3"))
91	
92	# Hybrid Evidence: numbered source passages instead of claim questions
93	# STORM/OpenScholar/Attribute-First pattern (ACL 2024-2026).
94	# When ON: evidence grouped by theme with [N] markers, LLM cites by
95	# number, post-process maps [N] -> [CITE:ev_xxx].
96	_HYBRID_EVIDENCE = os.getenv("PG_HYBRID_EVIDENCE", "0") == "1"
97	
98	# Learnings extraction env vars
99	_LLM_LEARNINGS_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_LLM_LEARNINGS_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
100	_LEARNINGS_BATCH_SIZE = int(os.getenv("PG_LEARNINGS_BATCH_SIZE", "10"))
101	_LEARNINGS_BATCH_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_LEARNINGS_BATCH_TIMEOUT", "180"))
102	_LEARNINGS_MAX_CONCURRENCY = int(
103	    os.getenv("PG_LEARNINGS_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "3"),
104	)
105	
106	# Tool names Qwen is allowed to pick (for extraction from malformed JSON)
107	_KNOWN_TOOLS = {
108	    "extract_numeric_data", "query_evidence_sql", "statistical_summary",
109	    "comparison_table", "meta_analysis", "agreement_analysis",
110	    "execute_python", "rank_by_impact", "stop",
111	}
112	
113	# Wave 4: Domain terms excluded from parroting Jaccard to focus on structural
114	# words that indicate actual copying (PlagBench domain-term exclusion pattern).
115	# C6 fix: only truly universal academic stopwords, not domain-specific terms.
116	# Override via PG_DOMAIN_TERMS_EXTRA env var (comma-separated).
117	_DOMAIN_TERMS_BASE = frozenset({
118	    "results", "study", "research", "data", "method", "using", "based",
119	    "system", "applied", "found", "showed", "observed", "reported",
120	    "obtained", "measured", "determined", "compared", "evaluated",
121	    "analysis", "performance", "process", "demonstrated", "indicated",
122	    "significant", "respectively", "approximately", "conditions",
123	})
124	_extra = os.getenv("PG_DOMAIN_TERMS_EXTRA", "")
125	_DOMAIN_TERMS = _DOMAIN_TERMS_BASE | frozenset(
126	    w.strip().lower() for w in _extra.split(",") if w.strip()
127	)
128	
129	# Wave 3: Non-entity words filtered from entity extraction to avoid
130	# matching sentence-initial words that aren't real entities.
131	_NON_ENTITIES = frozenset({
132	    "The", "However", "Additionally", "Furthermore", "Moreover",
133	    "Although", "While", "Because", "Since", "When", "Where",
134	    "This", "That", "These", "Those", "Each", "Every", "Some",
135	    "Most", "Many", "Several", "Here", "There", "Both", "All",
136	    "Other", "Such", "Any", "One", "Two", "Three", "Four", "Five",
137	    "For", "From", "With", "Into", "Over", "Under", "About",
138	    "After", "Before", "During", "Between", "Through", "Among",
139	})
140	
141	# P4: Hyphenated compound prefixes — words that are NOT standalone entities
142	# when they appear as the first component of a hyphenated compound
143	# (e.g., "Cross-linked" → should return "Cross-linked", not "Cross").
144	_ENTITY_PREFIXES = frozenset({
145	    "Cross", "Pre", "Post", "Non", "Sub", "Multi",
146	    "Over", "Under", "Out", "Re",
147	})
148	
149	# P0: RCS template echo patterns — unambiguous template leakage.
150	# CR5: only match "performs regarding" (clear template echo) and
151	# "role in X regarding" (from the replacement template if it leaks).
152	# "demonstrates significant" is NOT matched — it's valid analytical prose.
153	_TEMPLATE_ECHO = re.compile(
154	    r'[^.!?]*\b(?:performs?\s+regarding\b|'
155	    r'\brole\s+in\s+\w+\s+regarding\b)[^.!?]*[.!?]',
156	    re.IGNORECASE,
157	)
158	
159	# P0 Fix 3: Filler "X demonstrates Y" sentences — only match when the
160	# entire sentence is "{Entity} demonstrates {vague adj} {vague noun}."
161	# with no specific data or citations.
162	_FILLER_DEMONSTRATES = re.compile(
163	    r'(?:^|(?<=[.!?]\s))[A-Z]\w+\s+demonstrates?\s+'
164	    r'(?:significant|important|notable)\s+\w+\s*\.',
165	    re.MULTILINE,
166	)
167	
168	# D2-FIX: Broader template-echo pattern — catches "Surface demonstrates
169	# surface modification via plasma" where the subject word echoes in the
170	# predicate.  Jaccard guard in _post_process_interpretation() prevents
171	# over-stripping valid prose.
172	_TEMPLATE_ECHO_DEMONSTRATES = re.compile(
173	    r'(?:^|(?<=[.!?]\s))'               # sentence boundary
174	    r'([A-Z][A-Za-z]+(?:\s+[A-Za-z]+)*'  # subject phrase (1+ words)
175	    r'\s+demonstrates?\s+'                # "demonstrate(s)"
176	    r'[^.!?]+[.!?])',                     # rest of sentence
177	    re.MULTILINE,
178	)
179	
180	# P1: Synonym substitution table (BloomScrub pattern, arxiv:2504.16046).
181	# CR2: ONLY non-technical connectors/adverbs. Domain terms (removal,
182	# concentration, treatment, membrane, etc.) are EXCLUDED — swapping
183	# them changes technical meaning.
184	_SYNONYM_TABLE: dict[str, str] = {
185	    # Verbs (non-domain-specific)
186	    "achieves": "attains", "demonstrates": "exhibits",
187	    "shows": "reveals", "indicates": "suggests",
188	    "provides": "delivers", "utilizes": "employs",
189	    "obtained": "recorded", "observed": "noted",
190	    "reported": "documented", "conducted": "performed",
191	    # Adjectives (non-domain)
192	    "significant": "substantial", "important": "critical",
193	    "promising": "encouraging", "excellent": "outstanding",
194	    "various": "diverse", "suitable": "appropriate",
195	    "traditional": "conventional", "enhanced": "augmented",
196	    "rapid": "swift", "superior": "preferable",
197	    # Adverbs
198	    "relatively": "comparatively", "currently": "presently",
199	    "typically": "generally", "widely": "broadly",
200	    "primarily": "chiefly", "approximately": "roughly",
201	    # Nouns (non-domain connectors only)
202	    "advantages": "benefits", "challenges": "difficulties",
203	    "limitation": "constraint", "approach": "methodology",
204	}
205	
206	# P1: Verbatim-required patterns — skip synonym rewrite for these.
207	_VERBATIM_REQUIRED = re.compile(
208	    r'US\s+\d+,\d+|'                                    # Patent refs
209	    r'\$\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:billion|million|trillion)|'       # Dollar figures
210	    r'(?:\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|mg|nm|µm|°C|kWh|MPa)){3,}',  # 3+ nums+units
211	)
212	
213	# P2: Citation validation threshold (cosine similarity).
214	# CR3: 0.15 is derived from stress test data — FAIL cases had
215	# sem=-0.04 and sem=0.15, all PASS cases had sem>=0.41.
216	_CITE_VALIDATION_THRESHOLD = float(
217	    os.getenv("PG_CITE_VALIDATION_THRESHOLD", "0.15"),
218	)
219	
220	# R5: Legitimate doubled words (preserved by PDF artifact repair).
221	_R5_LEGIT_DOUBLES = frozenset({"had", "that", "can", "the"})
222	
223	# R6: Scientific lens context words (skip scrubbing near these).
224	_R6_SCI_LENS_WORDS = frozenset({
225	    "optical", "convex", "concave", "camera", "microscope",
226	    "zoom", "contact", "objective", "focal", "crystalline",
227	    "fisheye", "achromatic", "telephoto",
228	})
229	
230	# R3: Scale transformation words (billion, million, etc.).
231	_R3_SCALE_WORDS = frozenset({
232	    "billion", "million", "trillion", "thousand", "bn", "mn", "tn",
233	})
234	
235	# R7: Known transitive verbs for active-to-passive transform.
236	# Only these verbs are accepted — prevents misidentifying
237	# nouns/adjectives as verbs (CRITICAL-1 fix).
238	_R7_TRANSITIVE_VERBS = frozenset({
239	    "achieve", "show", "provide", "remove", "demonstrate",
240	    "exhibit", "indicate", "suggest", "reveal", "produce",
241	    "generate", "require", "enable", "reduce", "increase",
242	    "enhance", "maintain", "offer", "display", "yield",
243	    "cause", "create", "support", "facilitate", "ensure",
244	    "obtain", "prevent", "improve", "determine", "measure",
245	    "report", "describe", "confirm", "establish", "represent",
246	})
247	
248	# R7: Irregular past participles for passive voice transform.
249	_R7_IRREGULAR_PP = {
250	    "show": "shown", "give": "given", "take": "taken",
251	    "make": "made", "find": "found", "get": "gotten",
252	    "keep": "kept", "know": "known", "see": "seen",
253	    "write": "written", "drive": "driven", "break": "broken",
254	    "choose": "chosen", "grow": "grown", "speak": "spoken",
255	    "wear": "worn", "begin": "begun", "run": "run",
256	    "become": "become", "come": "come", "hold": "held",
257	    "lead": "led", "build": "built", "send": "sent",
258	    "spend": "spent", "leave": "left", "bring": "brought",
259	    "buy": "bought", "catch": "caught", "teach": "taught",
260	    "think": "thought", "seek": "sought", "tell": "told",
261	    "sell": "sold", "stand": "stood", "lose": "lost",
262	    "pay": "paid", "meet": "met", "set": "set", "cut": "cut",
263	    "put": "put", "read": "read", "hit": "hit",
264	}
265	
266	# R7: Words ending in 's' that are NOT plural (skip "are" logic).
267	_R7_SINGULAR_S = frozenset({
268	    "analysis", "process", "stress", "loss", "access",
269	    "success", "mass", "class", "glass", "gas", "basis",
270	    "thesis", "crisis", "diagnosis", "hypothesis", "synthesis",
271	    "apparatus", "status", "consensus", "focus", "radius",
272	})
273	
274	# Wave 2: Metric category lookup for analytical claim generation
275	_METRIC_CATEGORIES = [
276	    (re.compile(r'%'), "efficiency/rate"),
277	    (re.compile(r'mg/[Ll]|ppt|ppb|ppm|µg/[Ll]|ng/[Ll]'), "concentration level"),
278	    (re.compile(r'\$|cost|price|USD'), "cost metric"),
279	    (re.compile(r'kWh|MWh|energy'), "energy requirement"),
280	    (re.compile(r'µm|nm|mm|cm'), "particle/pore size"),
281	    (re.compile(r'MPa|GPa|kPa|PSI'), "mechanical strength"),
282	    (re.compile(r'°C|°F|kelvin'), "temperature condition"),
283	    (re.compile(r'[Ll]/min|m³/h|GPD|gallon'), "flow rate"),
284	]
285	
286	
287	class ReactDecision(BaseModel):
288	    """LLM's decision on what to do next in the ReAct loop.
289	
290	    Qwen 3.5 Plus sometimes returns simplified JSON that doesn't match
291	    the schema exactly (e.g. {"tool": "extract_numeric_data"} instead
292	    of {"reasoning": "...", "action": "..."}). The model_validator
293	    normalizes common deviations.
294	    """
295	
296	    reasoning: str = Field(
297	        description="Your reasoning for choosing this tool",
298	        default="",
299	    )
300	    action: str = Field(
301	        description="Tool name to execute, or 'stop' to finish analysis",
302	    )
303	    action_input: dict = Field(
304	        description="Parameters for the tool (empty dict if none needed)",
305	        default_factory=dict,
306	    )
307	
308	    @model_validator(mode="before")
309	    @classmethod
310	    def normalize_qwen_response(cls, data):
311	        """Handle common Qwen deviations from the expected schema.
312	
313	        Known patterns:
314	        - {"tool": "xxx"} instead of {"action": "xxx"}
315	        - {"action": {"tool": "xxx"}} nested dict
316	        - {"next_step": "xxx"} alternate field name
317	        - Missing "reasoning" field entirely
318	        - {"thought": "..."} instead of {"reasoning": "..."}
319	        """
320	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
321	            return data
322	
323	        # Normalize "tool" -> "action"
324	        for alt in ("tool", "tool_name", "next_step", "next_action", "name"):
325	            if alt in data and "action" not in data:
326	                data["action"] = data.pop(alt)
327	
328	        # Unwrap nested {"action": {"tool": "xxx"}}
329	        if isinstance(data.get("action"), dict):
330	            nested = data["action"]
331	            tool_name = (
332	                nested.get("tool")
333	                or nested.get("name")
334	                or nested.get("tool_name")
335	                or ""
336	            )
337	            if tool_name:
338	                data["action"] = str(tool_name)
339	            else:
340	                # Last resort: find any known tool name in the dict values
341	                for v in nested.values():
342	                    if isinstance(v, str) and v in _KNOWN_TOOLS:
343	                        data["action"] = v
344	                        break
345	                else:
346	                    data["action"] = "stop"
347	
348	        # Normalize "thought"/"explanation"/"reason" -> "reasoning"
349	        for alt in ("thought", "explanation", "reason", "thinking",
350	                     "rationale"):
351	            if alt in data and "reasoning" not in data:
352	                data["reasoning"] = data.pop(alt)
353	
354	        # Default reasoning if missing
355	        if "reasoning" not in data or not data["reasoning"]:
356	            action = data.get("action", "unknown")
357	            data["reasoning"] = f"Selected {action}"
358	
359	        # Normalize "params"/"parameters"/"input"/"args" -> "action_input"
360	        for alt in ("params", "parameters", "input", "args",
361	                     "tool_input", "kwargs"):
362	            if alt in data and "action_input" not in data:
363	                data["action_input"] = data.pop(alt)
364	
365	        return data
366	
367	    @field_validator("action", mode="before")
368	    @classmethod
369	    def coerce_action(cls, v):
370	        """Coerce action to string, handling unexpected types."""
371	        if isinstance(v, str):
372	            return v.strip().lower()
373	        if isinstance(v, dict):
374	            # {"tool": "xxx"} pattern
375	            return str(
376	                v.get("tool") or v.get("name") or v.get("action") or "stop"
377	            ).strip().lower()
378	        return str(v).strip().lower() if v else "stop"
379

381	class PlannedStep(BaseModel):
382	    """A single planned analysis step for the agentic pipeline.
383	
384	    Qwen 3.5 Plus returns simplified JSON — the model_validator
385	    normalizes common deviations (same pattern as ReactDecision).
386	    """
387	
388	    tool_name: str = Field(description="Tool to execute")
389	    reasoning: str = Field(
390	        description="Why this tool should run", default="",
391	    )
392	    parameters: dict = Field(
393	        description="Tool parameters", default_factory=dict,
394	    )
395	
396	    @model_validator(mode="before")
397	    @classmethod
398	    def normalize_qwen_response(cls, data):
399	        """Handle common Qwen deviations from the expected schema."""
400	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
401	            return data
402	        for alt in ("tool", "name", "action", "step", "tool_id"):
403	            if alt in data and "tool_name" not in data:
404	                data["tool_name"] = data.pop(alt)
405	        for alt in ("thought", "explanation", "reason", "rationale", "why"):
406	            if alt in data and "reasoning" not in data:
407	                data["reasoning"] = data.pop(alt)
408	        for alt in ("params", "args", "input", "kwargs", "action_input"):
409	            if alt in data and "parameters" not in data:
410	                data["parameters"] = data.pop(alt)
411	        if "reasoning" not in data or not data.get("reasoning"):
412	            data["reasoning"] = f"Run {data.get('tool_name', 'unknown')}"
413	        return data
414	
415	    @field_validator("tool_name", mode="before")
416	    @classmethod
417	    def coerce_tool_name(cls, v):
418	        """Coerce tool_name to lowercase string."""
419	        if isinstance(v, str):
420	            return v.strip().lower()
421	        if isinstance(v, dict):
422	            return str(
423	                v.get("tool") or v.get("name") or "extract_numeric_data"
424	            ).strip().lower()
425	        return str(v).strip().lower() if v else "extract_numeric_data"

428	class AnalysisPlan(BaseModel):
429	    """LLM's analysis plan — ordered list of tools to execute.
430	
431	    The agentic pipeline asks for ONE plan upfront (ReWOO pattern),
432	    then executes all steps deterministically without further LLM calls.
433	    """
434	
435	    steps: list[PlannedStep] = Field(
436	        description="Ordered list of analysis steps",
437	    )
438	
439	    @model_validator(mode="before")
440	    @classmethod
441	    def normalize_qwen_response(cls, data):
442	        """Handle common Qwen deviations from the expected schema."""
443	        # Handle bare list: Qwen returns ["tool1", "tool2"] or [{"tool": ...}]
444	        if isinstance(data, list):
445	            data = {"steps": data}
446	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
447	            return data
448	        for alt in ("plan", "tools", "actions", "tool_sequence", "sequence",
449	                     "analysis_steps", "ordered_steps"):
450	            if alt in data and "steps" not in data:
451	                data["steps"] = data.pop(alt)
452	        # Handle flat tool list: ["extract_numeric_data", "statistical_summary"]
453	        if isinstance(data.get("steps"), list) and data["steps"]:
454	            if isinstance(data["steps"][0], str):
455	                data["steps"] = [{"tool_name": t} for t in data["steps"]]
456	        return data

463	class CritiqueDimension(BaseModel):
464	    """Single dimension of the interpretation critique."""
465	
466	    dimension: str = Field(description="Dimension name")
467	    passed: bool = Field(
468	        description="Whether this dimension passes", default=False,
469	    )
470	    issues: list[str] = Field(
471	        description="Specific problems found", default_factory=list,
472	    )
473	
474	    @model_validator(mode="before")
475	    @classmethod
476	    def normalize_qwen_response(cls, data):
477	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
478	            return data
479	        for alt in ("name", "dim", "category", "dimension_name"):
480	            if alt in data and "dimension" not in data:
481	                data["dimension"] = data.pop(alt)
482	        for alt in ("pass", "ok", "passed", "status", "result",
483	                     "verdict"):
484	            if alt in data and "passed" not in data:
485	                data["passed"] = data.pop(alt)
486	        # Coerce string "PASS"/"FAIL" → bool
487	        if isinstance(data.get("passed"), str):
488	            data["passed"] = data["passed"].upper() in (
489	                "PASS", "TRUE", "YES", "OK", "PASSED",
490	            )
491	        for alt in ("problems", "findings", "errors"):
492	            if alt in data and "issues" not in data:
493	                data["issues"] = data.pop(alt)
494	        return data

496	
497	class InterpretationCritique(BaseModel):
498	    """Structured critique of an interpretation across 5 dimensions."""
499	
500	    dimensions: list[CritiqueDimension] = Field(
501	        description="Critique per dimension",
502	    )
503	    needs_rewrite: bool = Field(
504	        description="Whether the interpretation needs rewriting",
505	    )
506	    rewrite_instructions: str = Field(
507	        description="Specific fix instructions for the rewriter",
508	        default="",
509	    )
510	
511	    @model_validator(mode="before")
512	    @classmethod
513	    def normalize_qwen_response(cls, data):
514	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
515	            return data
516	        # Unwrap nested wrappers: Qwen sometimes wraps in
517	        # {"interpretation_critique": {actual_fields...}}
518	        for wrapper_key in list(data.keys()):
519	            val = data[wrapper_key]
520	            if (
521	                isinstance(val, dict)
522	                and len(data) == 1
523	                and any(
524	                    k in val
525	                    for k in ("dimensions", "needs_rewrite", "evaluation",
526	                              "dimension_evaluations")
527	                )
528	            ):
529	                data = val
530	                break
531	        # Catch-all: ANY key containing a list of dicts → dimensions
532	        # Qwen invents new field names every run.
533	        if "dimensions" not in data:
534	            for key, val in list(data.items()):
535	                if (
536	                    isinstance(val, list)
537	                    and val
538	                    and isinstance(val[0], dict)
539	                    and key not in ("needs_rewrite", "rewrite_instructions")
540	                ):
541	                    data["dimensions"] = data.pop(key)
542	                    break
543	            else:
544	                # Qwen returned evaluation as a single dict instead of
545	                # list — convert dict values to dimension list
546	                for key, val in list(data.items()):
547	                    if (
548	                        isinstance(val, dict)
549	                        and key not in (
550	                            "needs_rewrite", "rewrite_instructions",
551	                        )
552	                        and any(
553	                            isinstance(v, str)
554	                            for v in val.values()
555	                        )
556	                    ):
557	                        # {"sub_question_coverage": "PASS", ...}
558	                        dims = [
559	                            {"dimension": k, "passed": v}
560	                            for k, v in val.items()
561	                        ]
562	                        data["dimensions"] = dims
563	                        del data[key]
564	                        break
565	        for alt in ("rewrite", "needs_revision", "should_rewrite",
566	                     "verdict"):
567	            if alt in data and "needs_rewrite" not in data:
568	                val = data.pop(alt)
569	                # Coerce string verdicts like "PASS"/"FAIL" to bool
570	                if isinstance(val, str):
571	                    val = val.upper() in ("PASS", "TRUE", "YES", "OK")
572	                data["needs_rewrite"] = val
573	        for alt in ("instructions", "fix_instructions", "fixes"):
574	            if alt in data and "rewrite_instructions" not in data:
575	                data["rewrite_instructions"] = data.pop(alt)
576	        # Coerce None/list → "" for rewrite_instructions
577	        if not isinstance(data.get("rewrite_instructions"), str):
578	            data["rewrite_instructions"] = str(
579	                data.get("rewrite_instructions") or ""
580	            )
581	        return data
582

637	class ReactAnalysisAgent:
638	    """ReAct loop that autonomously analyzes evidence using registered tools.
639	
640	    Usage:
641	        agent = ReactAnalysisAgent(client, evidence_store, evidence_ids, query)
642	        notebook = await agent.run()
643	        entries = notebook.to_entries()
644	    """
645	
646	    def __init__(
647	        self,
648	        client,
649	        evidence_store: dict,
650	        evidence_ids: list[str],
651	        query: str,
652	        registry: ToolRegistry | None = None,
653	        tracer=None,
654	        mode: str | None = None,
655	    ):
656	        self._client = client
657	        self._evidence_store = evidence_store
658	        self._evidence_ids = evidence_ids
659	        self._query = query
660	        self._registry = registry or build_default_registry()
661	        # Hybrid evidence passage state
662	        self._passage_reverse_map: dict[int, str] = {}
663	        self._evidence_passages_text: str = ""
664	        self._tracer = tracer
665	        self._notebook = AnalysisNotebook(query, evidence_ids)
666	        # Mode precedence: PG_REACT_MODE env (legacy compat) > constructor
667	        # > PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE env (pipeline default) > "8phase"
668	        react_mode = os.getenv("PG_REACT_MODE", "")
669	        if react_mode:
670	            self._mode = react_mode
671	        elif mode:
672	            self._mode = mode
673	        else:
674	            self._mode = os.getenv("PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE", "") or "8phase"
675	
676	    async def run(self) -> AnalysisNotebook:
677	        """Execute analysis and return the notebook.
678	
679	        Dispatches based on pipeline mode:
680	        - "8phase": Plan→Execute→Briefing→Scaffold→Write→Critique→Rewrite→Verify
681	        - "legacy"/"agentic": Plan→Execute→Interpret→Verify (2 LLM calls)
682	        - "react": Per-step LLM decisions
683	        """
684	        if self._mode == "react":
685	            return await self._run_react()
686	        if self._mode in ("legacy", "agentic"):
687	            return await self._run_agentic_analysis()
688	        return await self._run_8phase_analysis()
689	
690	    async def _run_react(self) -> AnalysisNotebook:
691	        """Execute the ReAct loop (legacy mode)."""
692	        start_time = time.monotonic()
693	        max_iterations = _MAX_ITERATIONS
694	        timeout = _TIMEOUT_SECONDS
695	
696	        logger.info(
697	            "[react] Starting analysis: %d evidence, max_iter=%d, timeout=%ds",
698	            len(self._evidence_ids), max_iterations, timeout,
699	        )
700	
701	        for iteration in range(1, max_iterations + 1):
702	            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
703	
704	            # Budget check
705	            if elapsed >= timeout:
706	                logger.info(
707	                    "[react] Timeout reached after %.1fs, stopping", elapsed,
708	                )
709	                break
710	
711	            # Decide next action (retry once on failure)
712	            decision = None
713	            for attempt in range(2):
714	                try:
715	                    decision = await self._decide(iteration)
716	                    break
717	                except Exception as exc:
718	                    logger.warning(
719	                        "[react] Decision attempt %d failed at iter %d: %s "
720	                        "(%s)",
721	                        attempt + 1, iteration,
722	                        type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
723	                    )
724	                    if attempt == 0:
725	                        await asyncio.sleep(1)  # Brief pause before retry
726	
727	            if decision is None:
728	                logger.warning(
729	                    "[react] Decision failed after 2 attempts at iter %d, "
730	                    "running fallback",
731	                    iteration,
732	                )
733	                await self._run_fallback()
734	                break
735	
736	            if decision.action == "stop":
737	                logger.info(
738	                    "[react] LLM chose to stop: %s", decision.reasoning[:100],
739	                )
740	                break
741	
742	            # Execute the chosen tool
743	            step = await self._execute_tool(iteration, decision)
744	            self._notebook.add_step(step)
745	
746	            logger.info(
747	                "[react] Step %d: %s [%s] %.1fs — %s",
748	                iteration,
749	                decision.action,
750	                "OK" if step.result.success else "FAIL",
751	                step.elapsed_seconds,
752	                decision.reasoning[:60],
753	            )
754	
755	            # Check sufficiency
756	            if self._is_sufficient():
757	                logger.info("[react] Sufficient analysis achieved, stopping")
758	                break
759	
760	        # If no steps succeeded, run fallback
761	        if self._notebook.successful_steps == 0:
762	            logger.warning("[react] No successful steps, running fallback")
763	            await self._run_fallback()
764	
765	        # POST-PROCESSING: LLM interprets raw results into real insights
766	        # This is what separates "regex + scipy" from "analyst with reasoning"
767	        if self._notebook.successful_steps > 0 and self._client:
768	            await self._interpret_results()
769	
770	        total_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
771	        logger.info(
772	            "[react] Analysis complete: %d steps (%d ok), %d data points, "
773	            "%.1fs",
774	            self._notebook.step_count,
775	            self._notebook.successful_steps,
776	            len(self._notebook.data_points),
777	            total_elapsed,
778	        )
779	
780	        return self._notebook
781	
782	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
783	    # Agentic pipeline (Plan -> Execute -> Interpret -> Verify)
784	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
785	
786	    async def _run_agentic_analysis(self) -> AnalysisNotebook:
787	        """Execute Plan -> Execute -> Interpret -> Verify pipeline.
788	
789	        Phase 1: PLAN — 1 generate_structured() call produces AnalysisPlan
790	        Phase 2: EXECUTE — deterministic tool execution, 0 LLM calls
791	        Phase 3: INTERPRET — 1 generate() call produces analytical prose
792	        Phase 4: VERIFY — programmatic claim<->evidence check, 0 LLM calls
793	        """
794	        start_time = time.monotonic()
795	        timeout = _TIMEOUT_SECONDS
796	
797	        logger.info(
798	            "[agentic] Starting analysis: %d evidence, timeout=%ds",
799	            len(self._evidence_ids), timeout,
800	        )
801	
802	        # Phase 1: PLAN (1 LLM call, ~75s)
803	        plan = None
804	        try:
805	            plan = await self._plan_analysis()
806	            logger.info(
807	                "[agentic] Plan: %s",
808	                [s.tool_name for s in plan.steps],
809	            )
810	        except Exception as exc:
811	            logger.warning(
812	                "[agentic] Plan failed: %s: %s, using fallback plan",
813	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
814	            )
815	
816	        if not plan or not plan.steps:
817	            plan = AnalysisPlan(steps=[
818	                PlannedStep(
819	                    tool_name="extract_numeric_data",
820	                    reasoning="Always extract first",
821	                ),
822	                PlannedStep(
823	                    tool_name="statistical_summary",
824	                    reasoning="Compute statistics on extracted data",
825	                ),
826	                PlannedStep(
827	                    tool_name="query_evidence_sql",
828	                    reasoning="Get tier distribution and metadata",
829	                ),
830	            ])
831	            logger.info(
832	                "[agentic] Using fallback plan: %s",
833	                [s.tool_name for s in plan.steps],
834	            )
835	
836	        # Phase 2: EXECUTE (0 LLM calls, ~10s)
837	        for step_def in plan.steps:
838	            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
839	            if elapsed >= timeout:
840	                logger.info(
841	                    "[agentic] Timeout after %.1fs, stopping execution",
842	                    elapsed,
843	                )
844	                break
845	
846	            tool_def = self._registry.get_tool(step_def.tool_name)
847	            if not tool_def or not tool_def.execute:
848	                logger.warning(
849	                    "[agentic] Skipping unknown tool: %s",
850	                    step_def.tool_name,
851	                )
852	                continue
853	
854	            # Skip data-requiring tools if no data yet
855	            if tool_def.requires_data and not self._notebook.has_data:
856	                logger.info(
857	                    "[agentic] Skipping %s (requires data, none yet)",
858	                    step_def.tool_name,
859	                )
860	                continue
861	
862	            step_start = time.monotonic()
863	            try:
864	                result = await asyncio.wait_for(
865	                    tool_def.execute(
866	                        evidence_store=self._evidence_store,
867	                        data_points=self._notebook.data_points,
868	                        client=self._client,
869	                        **step_def.parameters,
870	                    ),
871	                    timeout=_TOOL_TIMEOUT,
872	                )
873	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
874	                result = ToolResult(
875	                    success=False,
876	                    tool_name=step_def.tool_name,
877	                    markdown=f"Tool timed out after {_TOOL_TIMEOUT}s",
878	                    error=f"Timeout after {_TOOL_TIMEOUT}s",
879	                )
880	            except Exception as exc:
881	                result = ToolResult(
882	                    success=False,
883	                    tool_name=step_def.tool_name,
884	                    markdown=f"Tool error: {str(exc)[:200]}",
885	                    error=str(exc)[:500],
886	                )
887	
888	            step_elapsed = time.monotonic() - step_start
889	            step = AnalysisStep(
890	                step_number=self._notebook.step_count + 1,
891	                reasoning=step_def.reasoning or f"Planned: {step_def.tool_name}",
892	                tool_name=step_def.tool_name,
893	                result=result,
894	                elapsed_seconds=round(step_elapsed, 3),
895	            )
896	            self._notebook.add_step(step)
897	
898	            logger.info(
899	                "[agentic] Step %d: %s [%s] %.1fs",
900	                step.step_number,
901	                step_def.tool_name,
902	                "OK" if result.success else "FAIL",
903	                step_elapsed,
904	            )
905	
906	        # If no steps succeeded, run deterministic fallback
907	        if self._notebook.successful_steps == 0:
908	            logger.warning("[agentic] No successful steps, running fallback")
909	            await self._run_fallback()
910	
911	        # Phase 3: INTERPRET (1 LLM call, ~120s)
912	        if self._notebook.successful_steps > 0 and self._client:
913	            await self._interpret_results()
914	
915	        # Phase 4: VERIFY (programmatic, ~5s)
916	        verification = self._verify_claims()
917	        if verification:
918	            logger.info(
919	                "[agentic] Verification: %d/%d claims verified, "
920	                "%d mismatches",
921	                verification.get("verified", 0),
922	                verification.get("total_claims_checked", 0),
923	                verification.get("mismatches", 0),
924	            )
925	
926	        total_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
927	        logger.info(
928	            "[agentic] Complete: %d steps (%d ok), %d data points, %.1fs",
929	            self._notebook.step_count,
930	            self._notebook.successful_steps,
931	            len(self._notebook.data_points),
932	            total_elapsed,
933	        )
934	
935	        return self._notebook
936	
937	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
938	    # 8-phase pipeline (Briefing→Scaffold→Write→Critique→Rewrite→Verify)
939	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
940	
941	    async def _run_8phase_analysis(self) -> AnalysisNotebook:
942	        """Execute analysis pipeline (6-phase adaptive or 8-phase legacy).
943	
944	        When PG_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD=1 (default):
945	          Phase 1: PLAN       — 1 generate_structured() call
946	          Phase 2: EXECUTE    — deterministic tool execution, 0 LLM calls
947	          Phase 3: BRIEFING+CLASSIFY — cluster + archetype detection
948	          Phase 4: SCAFFOLD   — 1 reason(): intent brief + 5-lens + gap queries
949	          Phase 5: GAP FILL   — 0 LLM: embedding search for missing evidence
950	          Phase 6: WRITE+REFINE — 2-4 generate(): draft + SELF-REFINE loop
951	          Phase 6.5: POST-POLISH — programmatic cleanup
952	          Phase 7: VERIFY     — programmatic claim verification
953	
954	        When PG_ADAPTIVE_SCAFFOLD=0 (legacy):
955	          Phases 5-7 use separate write→critique→rewrite flow.
956	        """
957	        start_time = time.monotonic()
958	        timeout = _TIMEOUT_SECONDS
959	        # INF-3: Per-phase cost/time tracking
960	        phase_timings: dict[str, float] = {}
961	        phase_costs: dict[str, float] = {}
962	
963	        def _snap_cost() -> float:
964	            """Snapshot current cost from client usage tracker."""

1396	    @staticmethod
1397	    def _distill_fact(statement: str) -> str:
1398	        """Distill an evidence statement to a concise fact (~20 words).
1399	
1400	        Strips common boilerplate prefixes and truncates to ~20 words.
1401	        Pure regex, no LLM call.
1402	        """
1403	        if not statement:
1404	            return ""
1405	
1406	        text = statement.strip()
1407	
1408	        # Strip boilerplate prefixes
1409	        boilerplate_patterns = [
1410	            r'^(?:the\s+)?(?:study|research|analysis|report|paper|'
1411	            r'investigation|review|assessment)\s+'
1412	            r'(?:found|showed|demonstrated|revealed|indicated|reported|'
1413	            r'concluded|suggests?|confirms?|determined)\s+that\s+',
1414	            r'^(?:according\s+to\s+(?:the|a|this)\s+'
1415	            r'(?:study|research|report|analysis|data|findings)),?\s*',
1416	            r'^(?:it\s+(?:was|is|has\s+been)\s+'
1417	            r'(?:found|shown|demonstrated|reported|observed)\s+that\s+)',
1418	            r'^(?:results?\s+(?:show|indicate|suggest|demonstrate)\s+that\s+)',
1419	            r'^(?:data\s+(?:shows?|indicates?|suggests?)\s+that\s+)',
1420	        ]
1421	        for pattern in boilerplate_patterns:
1422	            text = re.sub(pattern, "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
1423	
1424	        # Truncate to ~20 words
1425	        words = text.split()
1426	        if len(words) > 20:
1427	            text = " ".join(words[:20]) + "..."
1428	
1429	        return text.strip()
1430	
1431	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
1432	    # Learnings extraction (LLM-based, replaces regex _distill_fact)
1433	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
1434	
1435	    async def _extract_learnings_batch(
1436	        self,
1437	        evidence_batch: list[dict],
1438	    ) -> list[dict]:
1439	        """Extract learnings from a batch of evidence via free-form LLM.
1440	
1441	        Uses generate() (NOT generate_structured) — Qwen is much faster
1442	        without JSON schema enforcement. Parses markdown bullets with regex.
1443	        Falls back to _distill_fact() on failure.
1444	        """
1445	        if not self._client or not evidence_batch:
1446	            return self._fallback_distill_batch(evidence_batch)
1447	
1448	        evidence_lines = []
1449	        valid_ids = set()
1450	        for ev_dict in evidence_batch:
1451	            eid = ev_dict["eid"]
1452	            # 60 chars per item keeps prompt compact for large batches
1453	            stmt = ev_dict["statement"][:60]
1454	            valid_ids.add(eid)
1455	            evidence_lines.append(f"[{eid}]: {stmt}")
1456	
1457	        evidence_text = "\n".join(evidence_lines)
1458	
1459	        # Scale target learnings to batch size
1460	        target_min = max(10, len(evidence_batch) // 5)
1461	        target_max = max(20, len(evidence_batch) // 2)
1462	
1463	        prompt = (
1464	            f"RESEARCH: {self._query[:100]}\n\n"
1465	            f"EVIDENCE ({len(evidence_batch)} items):\n"
1466	            f"{evidence_text}\n\n"
1467	            f"Distill into {target_min}-{target_max} paraphrased "
1468	            f"learnings. Format EXACTLY:\n"
1469	            f"- [ev_xxx] (category) Paraphrased fact with numbers\n\n"
1470	            f"REPHRASE (never copy wording), keep numbers exact, "
1471	            f"merge duplicates. Categories: performance, cost, "
1472	            f"comparison, mechanism, limitation, application, general."
1473	        )
1474	
1475	        system = (
1476	            "Distill evidence into paraphrased bullet points. "
1477	            "Format: - [ev_xxx] (category) fact. Never copy wording."
1478	        )
1479	
1480	        # Learnings must fail fast — scaffold is what produces quality.
1481	        # Cap at 45s so 3 concurrent batches consume ≤45s total,
1482	        # leaving 500+ seconds for scaffold+write+critique.
1483	        # MUST pass to generate() — otherwise DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=90
1484	        # kills the httpx call internally.
1485	        batch_timeout = _LEARNINGS_BATCH_TIMEOUT  # default 45s
1486	
1487	        try:
1488	            response = await asyncio.wait_for(
1489	                self._client.generate(
1490	                    prompt=prompt,
1491	                    system=system,
1492	                    max_tokens=min(4096, len(evidence_batch) * 40),
1493	                    temperature=0.3,
1494	                    timeout=batch_timeout,
1495	                ),
1496	                timeout=batch_timeout + 30,
1497	            )
1498	
1499	            content = response.content.strip()
1500	            if not content:
1501	                return self._fallback_distill_batch(evidence_batch)
1502	
1503	            raw_learnings = self._parse_learning_bullets(
1504	                content, valid_ids,
1505	            )
1506	
1507	            if raw_learnings:
1508	                logger.info(
1509	                    "[8phase] LLM learnings: %d input -> %d learnings",
1510	                    len(evidence_batch), len(raw_learnings),
1511	                )
1512	                return raw_learnings
1513	
1514	            logger.warning(
1515	                "[8phase] LLM learnings parsed 0 from %d chars, "
1516	                "regex fallback",
1517	                len(content),
1518	            )
1519	            return self._fallback_distill_batch(evidence_batch)
1520	
1521	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
1522	            logger.warning(
1523	                "[8phase] LLM learnings timed out (%ds for %d ev), "
1524	                "regex fallback",
1525	                batch_timeout, len(evidence_batch),
1526	            )
1527	            return self._fallback_distill_batch(evidence_batch)
1528	        except Exception as exc:
1529	            logger.warning(
1530	                "[8phase] LLM learnings failed: %s: %s, regex fallback",
1531	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:300],
1532	            )
1533	            return self._fallback_distill_batch(evidence_batch)
1534	
1535	    def _fallback_distill_batch(
1536	        self, evidence_batch: list[dict],
1537	    ) -> list[dict]:
1538	        """Regex-based fallback for a failed LLM learnings batch."""
1539	        results = []
1540	        for ev_dict in evidence_batch:
1541	            fact = self._distill_fact(ev_dict["statement"])
1542	            if fact:
1543	                results.append({
1544	                    "fact": fact,
1545	                    "source_ids": [ev_dict["eid"]],
1546	                    "category": ev_dict["category"],
1547	                })
1548	        return results
1549	
1550	    async def _extract_all_learnings(self) -> list[dict]:
1551	        """Extract learnings from ALL evidence in a single LLM call.
1552	
1553	        Uses generate() with a compact evidence summary — one call for
1554	        all evidence instead of batched calls. Qwen's per-call overhead
1555	        (~6s routing + thinking) makes batching unviable.
1556	
1557	        Returns list of dicts compatible with _build_evidence_briefing.
1558	        """
1559	        evidence_items = []
1560	        for eid in self._evidence_ids:
1561	            ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
1562	            stmt = ev.get("statement", "")
1563	            if not stmt or len(stmt) < 10:
1564	                continue
1565	            evidence_items.append({
1566	                "eid": eid,
1567	                "statement": stmt,
1568	                "category": ev.get("fact_category", "general"),
1569	                "tier": ev.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE"),
1570	                "relevance": float(ev.get("relevance_score", 0.5)),
1571	                "perspective": ev.get("perspective", ""),
1572	            })
1573	
1574	        if not evidence_items:
1575	            return []
1576	
1577	        # Gate: LLM learnings disabled, no client, or evidence >100
1578	        # INF-1: Skip LLM for large evidence sets (timeout risk)
1579	        _learnings_llm_threshold = int(
1580	            os.getenv("PG_LEARNINGS_LLM_THRESHOLD", "100"),
1581	        )
1582	        if (
1583	            not _LLM_LEARNINGS_ENABLED
1584	            or not self._client
1585	            or len(evidence_items) > _learnings_llm_threshold
1586	        ):
1587	            logger.info(
1588	                "[8phase] LLM learnings skipped (%d ev, threshold=%d), "
1589	                "using regex",
1590	                len(evidence_items), _learnings_llm_threshold,
1591	            )
1592	            return self._build_learnings_from_regex(evidence_items)
1593	
1594	        # Split into ~3 large batches for concurrent extraction
1595	        # Qwen takes ~3s/item — 3 batches of 85 items concurrently ≈ 90s
1596	        batch_size = max(
1597	            _LEARNINGS_BATCH_SIZE,
1598	            (len(evidence_items) + 2) // 3,  # ~3 batches
1599	        )
1600	        batches = [
1601	            evidence_items[i:i + batch_size]
1602	            for i in range(0, len(evidence_items), batch_size)
1603	        ]
1604	
1605	        logger.info(
1606	            "[8phase] LLM learnings: %d evidence -> %d batches of ~%d",
1607	            len(evidence_items), len(batches), batch_size,
1608	        )
1609	
1610	        # Run batches concurrently
1611	        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(_LEARNINGS_MAX_CONCURRENCY)
1612	
1613	        async def _run_batch(batch):
1614	            async with sem:
1615	                return await self._extract_learnings_batch(batch)
1616	
1617	        batch_results = await asyncio.gather(
1618	            *[_run_batch(b) for b in batches],
1619	            return_exceptions=True,
1620	        )
1621	
1622	        # Merge results, regex fallback per failed batch
1623	        raw_learnings = []
1624	        for i, result in enumerate(batch_results):
1625	            if isinstance(result, Exception):
1626	                logger.warning(
1627	                    "[8phase] Batch %d failed (%s), regex fallback",
1628	                    i, str(result)[:100],
1629	                )
1630	                raw_learnings.extend(
1631	                    self._fallback_distill_batch(batches[i]),
1632	                )
1633	            elif isinstance(result, list):
1634	                raw_learnings.extend(result)
1635	            else:
1636	                raw_learnings.extend(
1637	                    self._fallback_distill_batch(batches[i]),
1638	                )
1639	
1640	        return self._enrich_learnings(raw_learnings, evidence_items)
1641	
1642	    def _parse_learning_bullets(
1643	        self,
1644	        content: str,
1645	        valid_ids: set[str],
1646	    ) -> list[dict]:
1647	        """Parse markdown bullet list into learning dicts.
1648	
1649	        Expected format: - [ev_xxx] (category) fact text
1650	        Also handles: - [ev_xxx, ev_yyy] (category) fact text
1651	        Tolerant of trailing commas, truncated IDs, missing parens.
1652	        """
1653	        learnings = []
1654	
1655	        # Primary pattern: - [ev_xxx] (category) fact
1656	        bullet_pattern = re.compile(
1657	            r'^[-*]\s*\[([^\]]+)\]\s*'      # evidence IDs in brackets
1658	            r'(?:\((\w+)\)\s*)?'             # optional category in parens
1659	            r'(.+)$',                         # fact text
1660	            re.MULTILINE,
1661	        )
1662	
1663	        for match in bullet_pattern.finditer(content):
1664	            ids_str = match.group(1)
1665	            category = (match.group(2) or "general").lower()
1666	            fact = match.group(3).strip()
1667	
1668	            # Extract all ev_xxx patterns from the IDs string
1669	            # (handles trailing commas, spaces, truncated hashes)
1670	            raw_ids = re.findall(r'ev_[a-f0-9]{6,}', ids_str)
1671	            validated_ids = [sid for sid in raw_ids if sid in valid_ids]
1672	
1673	            if not validated_ids or not fact or len(fact) < 10:
1674	                continue
1675	
1676	            learnings.append({
1677	                "fact": fact,
1678	                "source_ids": validated_ids,
1679	                "category": category,
1680	            })
1681	
1682	        return learnings
1683	
1684	    def _build_learnings_from_regex(
1685	        self, evidence_items: list[dict],
1686	    ) -> list[dict]:
1687	        """Build learnings via regex fallback for ALL evidence."""
1688	        results = []
1689	        for ev in evidence_items:
1690	            fact = self._distill_fact(ev["statement"])
1691	            if not fact:
1692	                continue
1693	            results.append({
1694	                "fact": fact,
1695	                "category": ev["category"],
1696	                "tier": ev["tier"],
1697	                "evidence_ids": [ev["eid"]],
1698	                "relevance": ev["relevance"],
1699	                "perspective": ev["perspective"],
1700	                "original_statement": ev["statement"],
1701	            })
1702	        return results
1703	
1704	    def _enrich_learnings(
1705	        self,
1706	        raw_learnings: list[dict],
1707	        evidence_items: list[dict],
1708	    ) -> list[dict]:
1709	        """Enrich LLM-extracted learnings with metadata from sources.
1710	
1711	        Maps source_ids back to tier/relevance/perspective, producing
1712	        dicts compatible with downstream consumers.
1713	        """
1714	        ev_lookup = {ev["eid"]: ev for ev in evidence_items}
1715	        tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
1716	
1717	        enriched = []
1718	        for learning in raw_learnings:
1719	            source_ids = learning.get(
1720	                "source_ids", learning.get("evidence_ids", []),
1721	            )
1722	            tier = "BRONZE"
1723	            relevance = 0.5
1724	            perspective = ""
1725	            original_stmt = ""
1726	            for sid in source_ids:
1727	                if sid in ev_lookup:
1728	                    src = ev_lookup[sid]
1729	                    if tier_order.get(src["tier"], 3) < tier_order.get(
1730	                        tier, 3,
1731	                    ):
1732	                        tier = src["tier"]
1733	                    relevance = max(relevance, src["relevance"])
1734	                    if not perspective:
1735	                        perspective = src["perspective"]
1736	                    if not original_stmt:
1737	                        original_stmt = src["statement"]
1738	
1739	            enriched.append({
1740	                "fact": learning["fact"],
1741	                "category": learning.get("category", "general"),
1742	                "tier": tier,
1743	                "evidence_ids": source_ids,
1744	                "relevance": relevance,
1745	                "perspective": perspective,
1746	                "original_statement": original_stmt,
1747	            })
1748	
1749	        return enriched
1750	
1751	    async def _build_evidence_briefing(self) -> dict:
1752	        """Build structured evidence briefing from ALL evidence (Phase 3).
1753	
1754	        Uses LLM-based learnings extraction (when enabled) to force
1755	        paraphrasing. Falls back to regex distillation on failure.
1756	
1757	        Returns:
1758	            {
1759	                "learnings": [{"fact": str, "category": str, "tier": str,
1760	                              "evidence_ids": [str], "relevance": float}],
1761	                "clusters": [{"theme": str, "learning_indices": [int],
1762	                             "evidence_count": int}],
1763	                "sub_questions": [str],
1764	                "comparison_matrix": str,
1765	            }
1766	        """
1767	        # Step 1: Extract learnings (LLM-based or regex fallback)
1768	        raw_learnings = await self._extract_all_learnings()
1769	
1770	        if not raw_learnings:
1771	            return {
1772	                "learnings": [],
1773	                "clusters": [],
1774	                "sub_questions": self._decompose_query(),
1775	                "comparison_matrix": "",
1776	            }
1777	
1778	        # Step 2: Domain filter via embedding similarity
1779	        try:
1780	            query_embedding = embed_text(self._query)
1781	            fact_texts = [l["fact"] for l in raw_learnings]
1782	            fact_embeddings = embed_texts(fact_texts)
1783	
1784	            query_vec = np.array(query_embedding)
1785	            filtered_learnings = []
1786	            filtered_embeddings = []
1787	            for i, learning in enumerate(raw_learnings):
1788	                fact_vec = np.array(fact_embeddings[i])
1789	                norm_q = np.linalg.norm(query_vec)
1790	                norm_f = np.linalg.norm(fact_vec)
1791	                if norm_q > 0 and norm_f > 0:
1792	                    cos_sim = float(
1793	                        np.dot(query_vec, fact_vec) / (norm_q * norm_f)
1794	                    )
1795	                else:
1796	                    cos_sim = 0.0
1797	
1798	                if cos_sim >= _DOMAIN_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD:
1799	                    learning["relevance"] = max(
1800	                        learning["relevance"], cos_sim,
1801	                    )
1802	                    filtered_learnings.append(learning)
1803	                    filtered_embeddings.append(fact_embeddings[i])
1804	
1805	            logger.info(
1806	                "[8phase] Domain filter: %d/%d learnings passed (threshold=%.2f)",
1807	                len(filtered_learnings), len(raw_learnings),
1808	                _DOMAIN_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD,
1809	            )
1810	        except Exception as exc:
1811	            logger.warning(
1812	                "[8phase] Embedding failed, skipping domain filter: %s",
1813	                str(exc)[:200],
1814	            )
1815	            filtered_learnings = raw_learnings
1816	            filtered_embeddings = []
1817	
1818	        if not filtered_learnings:
1819	            # If filter was too aggressive, keep top 50% by relevance
1820	            raw_learnings.sort(key=lambda x: x["relevance"], reverse=True)
1821	            filtered_learnings = raw_learnings[:max(5, len(raw_learnings) // 2)]
1822	            filtered_embeddings = []
1823	
1824	        # Step 3: Cluster by embedding similarity
1825	        clusters = self._cluster_learnings(
1826	            filtered_learnings, filtered_embeddings,
1827	        )
1828	
1829	        # Step 4: Decompose query into sub-questions
1830	        sub_questions = self._decompose_query()
1831	
1832	        # Step 5: Build comparison matrix
1833	        comparison_matrix = self._build_comparison_matrix(
1834	            filtered_learnings, sub_questions,
1835	        )
1836	
1837	        return {
1838	            "learnings": filtered_learnings,
1839	            "clusters": clusters,
1840	            "sub_questions": sub_questions,
1841	            "comparison_matrix": comparison_matrix,
1842	        }
1843	
1844	    def _cluster_learnings(
1845	        self,
1846	        learnings: list[dict],
1847	        embeddings: list[list[float]],
1848	    ) -> list[dict]:
1849	        """Cluster learnings by embedding similarity.
1850	
1851	        Uses scipy agglomerative clustering with cosine distance.
1852	        Falls back to category-based grouping if embeddings unavailable.
1853	        """
1854	        if len(learnings) <= 1:
1855	            if learnings:
1856	                return [{
1857	                    "theme": learnings[0].get("category", "general"),
1858	                    "learning_indices": [0],
1859	                    "evidence_count": 1,
1860	                }]
1861	            return []
1862	
1863	        # Try embedding-based clustering
1864	        if embeddings and len(embeddings) == len(learnings):
1865	            try:
1866	                emb_matrix = np.array(embeddings)
1867	                # Compute pairwise cosine distances
1868	                dists = pdist(emb_matrix, metric="cosine")
1869	                # Replace NaN distances (zero-norm vectors) with 1.0
1870	                dists = np.nan_to_num(dists, nan=1.0)
1871	                linkage_matrix = linkage(dists, method="average")
1872	                labels = fcluster(linkage_matrix, t=0.5, criterion="distance")
1873	
1874	                cluster_map: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
1875	                for idx, label in enumerate(labels):
1876	                    cluster_map.setdefault(int(label), []).append(idx)
1877	
1878	                clusters = []
1879	                for label in sorted(cluster_map.keys()):
1880	                    indices = cluster_map[label]
1881	                    # Label cluster by most common category
1882	                    cats = [
1883	                        learnings[i].get("category", "general")
1884	                        for i in indices
1885	                    ]
1886	                    theme = max(set(cats), key=cats.count)
1887	
1888	                    # Sort: GOLD first, then by relevance desc
1889	                    tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
1890	                    indices.sort(key=lambda i: (
1891	                        tier_order.get(learnings[i].get("tier", "BRONZE"), 3),
1892	                        -learnings[i].get("relevance", 0),
1893	                    ))
1894	
1895	                    # Cap per cluster
1896	                    capped = indices[:_LEARNINGS_PER_CLUSTER]
1897	                    clusters.append({
1898	                        "theme": theme,
1899	                        "learning_indices": capped,
1900	                        "evidence_count": len(capped),
1901	                    })
1902	
1903	                # Cap total clusters
1904	                clusters.sort(
1905	                    key=lambda c: c["evidence_count"], reverse=True,
1906	                )
1907	                return clusters[:_MAX_CLUSTERS]
1908	            except Exception as exc:
1909	                logger.warning(
1910	                    "[8phase] Clustering failed: %s, using category groups",
1911	                    str(exc)[:200],
1912	                )
1913	
1914	        # Fallback: group by category
1915	        cat_map: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
1916	        for i, learning in enumerate(learnings):
1917	            cat = learning.get("category", "general")
1918	            cat_map.setdefault(cat, []).append(i)
1919	
1920	        clusters = []
1921	        for cat, indices in cat_map.items():
1922	            # Sort by tier + relevance
1923	            tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
1924	            indices.sort(key=lambda i: (
1925	                tier_order.get(learnings[i].get("tier", "BRONZE"), 3),
1926	                -learnings[i].get("relevance", 0),
1927	            ))
1928	            clusters.append({
1929	                "theme": cat,
1930	                "learning_indices": indices[:_LEARNINGS_PER_CLUSTER],
1931	                "evidence_count": min(len(indices), _LEARNINGS_PER_CLUSTER),
1932	            })
1933	
1934	        clusters.sort(key=lambda c: c["evidence_count"], reverse=True)
1935	        return clusters[:_MAX_CLUSTERS]
1936	
1937	    def _decompose_query(self) -> list[str]:
1938	        """Decompose query into sub-questions via regex.
1939	
1940	        Detects multi-criteria patterns and generates targeted sub-questions.
1941	        """
1942	        query = self._query.lower()
1943	        sub_questions = []
1944	
1945	        # Pattern: "effective AND affordable" / "X and Y"
1946	        and_match = re.findall(
1947	            r'(\w+)\s+(?:and|&|as well as|plus)\s+(\w+)',
1948	            query, re.IGNORECASE,
1949	        )
1950	        for w1, w2 in and_match:
1951	            sub_questions.append(f"What is the {w1} of each option?")
1952	            sub_questions.append(f"What is the {w2} of each option?")
1953	
1954	        # Pattern: "compare X vs Y" / "X versus Y"
1955	        vs_match = re.findall(
1956	            r'compare\s+(.+?)\s+(?:vs|versus|with|against|to)\s+(.+?)(?:\s|$)',
1957	            query, re.IGNORECASE,
1958	        )
1959	        for entity1, entity2 in vs_match:
1960	            sub_questions.append(
1961	                f"What are the strengths of {entity1.strip()}?"
1962	            )
1963	            sub_questions.append(
1964	                f"What are the strengths of {entity2.strip()}?"
1965	            )
1966	
1967	        # Pattern: "most effective" / "best" → ranking question
1968	        if re.search(r'\b(?:most|best|top|leading|optimal)\b', query):
1969	            sub_questions.append(
1970	                "What is the evidence-based ranking of options?"
1971	            )
1972	
1973	        # Pattern: cost-related
1974	        if re.search(r'\b(?:cost|afford|cheap|expens|price|budget)\b', query):
1975	            sub_questions.append(
1976	                "What are the cost considerations for each option?"
1977	            )
1978	
1979	        # Pattern: effectiveness
1980	        if re.search(
1981	            r'\b(?:effect|efficien|remov|treat|perform|capab)\b', query,
1982	        ):
1983	            sub_questions.append(
1984	                "What is the effectiveness/performance of each option?"
1985	            )
1986	
1987	        # Always include a gaps question
1988	        sub_questions.append("What gaps remain in the evidence?")
1989	
1990	        # Deduplicate while preserving order
1991	        seen = set()
1992	        unique = []
1993	        for q in sub_questions:
1994	            q_lower = q.lower()
1995	            if q_lower not in seen:
1996	                seen.add(q_lower)
1997	                unique.append(q)
1998	
1999	        return unique

2000	
2001	    def _classify_query(self, briefing: dict) -> dict:
2002	        """Classify query archetype and determine required artifacts.
2003	
2004	        Returns dict with archetype, artifacts list, and evidence_signals.
2005	        Zero LLM calls — pure regex + evidence inspection.
2006	        """
2007	        query = self._query.lower()
2008	        learnings = briefing.get("learnings", [])
2009	
2010	        # Detect archetype via regex patterns
2011	        archetype = "general"
2012	        if re.search(
2013	            r'\b(?:compare|vs|versus|difference|between)\b', query,
2014	        ):
2015	            archetype = "comparison"
2016	        elif re.search(
2017	            r'\b(?:how\s+does|mechanism|why\s+does|process|pathway)\b',
2018	            query,
2019	        ):
2020	            archetype = "mechanism"
2021	        elif re.search(
2022	            r'\b(?:best|rank|top|most\s+effective|optimal|recommend)\b',
2023	            query,
2024	        ):
2025	            archetype = "ranking"
2026	        elif re.search(
2027	            r'\b(?:cost|price|afford|budget|economic|roi)\b', query,
2028	        ):
2029	            archetype = "cost_analysis"
2030	
2031	        # Evidence signal inspection
2032	        cost_learnings = sum(
2033	            1 for l in learnings
2034	            if re.search(
2035	                r'(?:cost|\$|price|USD|afford|budget)', l.get("fact", ""),
2036	                re.IGNORECASE,
2037	            )
2038	        )
2039	        numeric_learnings = sum(
2040	            1 for l in learnings
2041	            if re.search(r'\d+\.?\d*\s*%', l.get("fact", ""))
2042	        )
2043	        has_entities = bool(re.search(
2044	            r'(?:compare|vs|versus)\s+\w+', query,
2045	        ))
2046	        # Detect implicit multi-option queries (plural nouns =
2047	        # multiple options to compare even without "compare/vs")
2048	        implies_multiple = bool(re.search(
2049	            r'\b(?:technologies|methods|options|approaches|'
2050	            r'techniques|systems|materials|alternatives)\b',
2051	            query,
2052	        ))
2053	
2054	        # Determine required artifacts based on archetype + evidence
2055	        artifacts = []
2056	        # Comparison table: explicit comparison OR ranking queries
2057	        # that imply multiple options (e.g., "most effective
2058	        # technologies" = multiple techs to compare)
2059	        if (
2060	            archetype == "comparison"
2061	            or has_entities
2062	            or (archetype == "ranking" and implies_multiple)
2063	        ):
2064	            artifacts.append("comparison_table")
2065	        if archetype in ("ranking", "comparison"):
2066	            artifacts.append("evidence_based_ranking")
2067	        if archetype == "mechanism":
2068	            artifacts.append("mechanism_analysis")
2069	        if cost_learnings >= 2:
2070	            artifacts.append("cost_model")
2071	        # Conditional recommendations: ranking, comparison, and
2072	        # cost_analysis archetypes all benefit from If/Then guidance
2073	        if (
2074	            archetype in ("ranking", "comparison", "cost_analysis")
2075	            or cost_learnings >= 1
2076	        ):
2077	            artifacts.append("conditional_recommendations")
2078	        # decision_matrix removed: LLM fabricates numeric scores
2079	        # instead of filling the template. Evidence-based ranking
2080	        # table serves the same purpose without hallucination risk.
2081	
2082	        return {
2083	            "archetype": archetype,
2084	            "artifacts": artifacts,
2085	            "evidence_signals": {
2086	                "cost_learnings": cost_learnings,
2087	                "numeric_learnings": numeric_learnings,
2088	                "has_entities": has_entities,
2089	            },
2090	        }
2091	
2092	    def _build_comparison_matrix(
2093	        self,
2094	        learnings: list[dict],
2095	        sub_questions: list[str],
2096	    ) -> str:
2097	        """Build a markdown comparison matrix for multi-criteria queries.
2098	
2099	        Groups learnings by subject entity × criterion.
2100	        Returns empty string if query is single-criterion.
2101	        """
2102	        if len(sub_questions) < 3:
2103	            return ""
2104	
2105	        # Extract entity mentions from learnings
2106	        entities: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
2107	        for learning in learnings:
2108	            fact = learning["fact"].lower()
2109	            # Try to extract the subject (first capitalized noun phrase)
2110	            subject_match = re.search(
2111	                r'([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)',
2112	                learning["fact"],
2113	            )
2114	            if subject_match:
2115	                entity = subject_match.group(1)
2116	            else:
2117	                # Use category as fallback
2118	                entity = learning.get("category", "general")
2119	            entities.setdefault(entity, []).append(learning)
2120	
2121	        if len(entities) < 2:
2122	            return ""
2123	
2124	        # Build markdown table
2125	        # Columns: entity | key findings
2126	        lines = ["| Entity | Key Finding | Evidence |"]
2127	        lines.append("|--------|-------------|----------|")
2128	
2129	        for entity, entity_learnings in sorted(
2130	            entities.items(), key=lambda x: -len(x[1]),
2131	        )[:10]:
2132	            # Pick highest-relevance learning
2133	            best = max(entity_learnings, key=lambda l: l["relevance"])
2134	            ev_ids = ", ".join(best["evidence_ids"][:2])
2135	            lines.append(
2136	                f"| {entity} | {best['fact'][:80]} | {ev_ids} |"
2137	            )
2138	
2139	        return "\n".join(lines)

2145	    def _build_scaffold_prompt(

2148	        """Build 3-section scaffold prompt: intent + 5-lens + gap queries.

2158	        # PaperQA2 pattern: scaffold LLM sees questions, not raw facts
2159	        analytical_claims = self._build_analytical_claims(
2160	            briefing, top_n=_EVIDENCE_PER_CLUSTER,
2161	        )
2162	
2163	        archetype = (classification or {}).get("archetype", "general")
2164	        artifacts = (classification or {}).get("artifacts", [])
2165	        artifact_list = ", ".join(artifacts) if artifacts else "analytical prose"
2166	
2167	        # Build artifact-specific sections

2170	            artifact_sections.append(
2171	                "LENS 3 — COMPARATOR: Head-to-head analysis.\n"
