5050	    def _strip_phantom_citations(self, text: str) -> str:
5051	        """Remove citation tokens whose IDs are not in the evidence store.
5052	
5053	        Phantom citations are NEVER valid — they reference non-existent
5054	        evidence. Safe to run unconditionally on any draft.
5055	        """
5056	        all_cited = set(re.findall(r'\[CITE:([^\]]+)\]', text))
5057	        phantoms = [
5058	            c for c in all_cited if c not in self._evidence_store
5059	        ]
5060	        if phantoms:
5061	            for cid in phantoms:
5062	                text = text.replace(f"[CITE:{cid}]", "")
5063	            text = re.sub(r'\s*,\s*\.', '.', text)
5064	            text = re.sub(r'\s+\.', '.', text)
5065	            logger.info(
5066	                "[quality-gate] Stripped %d phantom citations: %s",
5067	                len(phantoms),
5068	                ", ".join(p[:16] for p in phantoms[:5]),
5069	            )
5070	        return text
5071	
5072	    def _compute_parroting_ratio(
5073	        self, text: str,
5074	    ) -> tuple[float, int]:
5075	        """Compute 3-gram Jaccard overlap between text and evidence.
5076	
5077	        Wave 4 (DEER + PlagBench):
5078	        - Domain-term exclusion: filter common domain words before
5079	          building n-grams to focus Jaccard on structural words.
5080	        - Cited-evidence-only: only check evidence IDs that appear as
5081	          [CITE:ev_xxx] in the sentence (DEER backreference pattern).
5082	        - Lower threshold: 0.50 → 0.35 (after domain exclusion).
5083	
5084	        Returns:
5085	            Tuple of (ratio, absolute_count) where ratio is
5086	            parroted/checked and absolute_count is the raw number
5087	            of parroted sentences.
5088	        """
5089	        sentences = re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', text)
5090	        if not sentences:
5091	            return 0.0, 0
5092	
5093	        cite_re = re.compile(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]')
5094	        # W4: configurable parroting Jaccard threshold
5095	        jaccard_threshold = float(
5096	            os.getenv("PG_PARROTING_JACCARD_THRESHOLD", "0.40"),
5097	        )
5098	        parroted = 0
5099	        checked = 0
5100	        for sent in sentences:
5101	            # Domain-term exclusion: filter before n-gram construction
5102	            sent_words = [
5103	                w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sent.lower())
5104	                if w not in _DOMAIN_TERMS
5105	            ]
5106	            if len(sent_words) < 5:
5107	                continue
5108	            checked += 1
5109	            # Build 3-grams
5110	            sent_ngrams = set()
5111	            for i in range(len(sent_words) - 2):
5112	                sent_ngrams.add(
5113	                    (sent_words[i], sent_words[i + 1], sent_words[i + 2]),
5114	                )
5115	            if not sent_ngrams:
5116	                continue
5117	
5118	            # DEER: only check evidence cited in THIS sentence
5119	            cited_eids = cite_re.findall(sent)
5120	            check_eids = cited_eids if cited_eids else (
5121	                self._evidence_ids[:100]
5122	            )
5123	
5124	            for eid in check_eids:
5125	                ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
5126	                ev_stmt = ev.get("statement", "")
5127	                ev_words = [
5128	                    w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ev_stmt.lower())
5129	                    if w not in _DOMAIN_TERMS
5130	                ]
5131	                ev_ngrams = set()
5132	                for i in range(len(ev_words) - 2):
5133	                    ev_ngrams.add(
5134	                        (ev_words[i], ev_words[i + 1], ev_words[i + 2]),
5135	                    )
5136	                if not ev_ngrams:
5137	                    continue
5138	                overlap = len(sent_ngrams & ev_ngrams)
5139	                union = len(sent_ngrams | ev_ngrams)
5140	                if union > 0 and overlap / union > jaccard_threshold:
5141	                    parroted += 1
5142	                    break
5143	
5144	        return parroted / max(checked, 1), parroted
5145	
5146	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5147	    # Wave 4: Structural sentence rewrite (replaces framing prefix)
5148	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5149	
5150	    @staticmethod
5151	    def _structural_rewrite(sentence: str) -> str:
5152	        """Rewrite a parroted sentence using structural transforms.
5153	
5154	        Wave 4 (Process-Supervised Rewrite, arxiv:2509.15577):
5155	        Two reliable transforms:
5156	        B — Numeric Foregrounding: move number+unit to sentence start
5157	        D — Causal Inversion: swap cause and effect clauses
5158	
5159	        Citation positions are preserved via placeholder swap.
5160	        """
5161	        # Step 1: Protect citations with placeholders
5162	        cite_re = re.compile(r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]')
5163	        cites = cite_re.findall(sentence)
5164	        working = sentence
5165	        placeholders = []
5166	        for i, cite in enumerate(cites):
5167	            placeholder = f"__CITE_{i}__"
5168	            placeholders.append((placeholder, cite))
5169	            working = working.replace(cite, placeholder, 1)
5170	
5171	        rewritten = working
5172	
5173	        # Transform B: Numeric Foregrounding (WP-1.1: gated, default OFF)
5174	        # Creates defects A2 ("2 GPa" orphan), A3 ("99.Achieving 0%").
5175	        # Disabled by default; re-enable via PG_TRANSFORM_B_ENABLED=1.
5176	        _transform_b = os.getenv("PG_TRANSFORM_B_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
5177	        if _transform_b:
5178	            num_match = re.search(
5179	                r'(\d+\.?\d*)\s*(%|mg/[Ll]|ppt|ppb|ppm|µm|nm|mm|cm|'
5180	                r'\$|kWh|MWh|MPa|GPa|°C|[Ll]/min|m³/h)',
5181	                working,
5182	            )
5183	        else:
5184	            num_match = None
5185	
5186	        if num_match:
5187	            number = num_match.group(1)
5188	            unit = num_match.group(2)
5189	
5190	            # Determine preposition based on unit
5191	            if unit == "$":
5192	                preposition = f"At a cost of ${number}"
5193	            elif unit == "%":
5194	                preposition = f"Achieving {number}%"
5195	            else:
5196	                preposition = f"At {number} {unit}"
5197	
5198	            # Lowercase the original sentence and prepend
5199	            body = working
5200	            if body and body[0].isupper():
5201	                body = body[0].lower() + body[1:]
5202	            # Strip terminal punctuation from body for clean join
5203	            body = body.rstrip(".")
5204	            rewritten = f"{preposition}, {body}."
5205	
5206	        elif re.search(
5207	            r'\bbecause\b|\bdue to\b|\bresulting from\b',
5208	            working, re.IGNORECASE,
5209	        ):
5210	            # Transform D: Causal Inversion
5211	            # "X is effective because Y" → "Y leads to effective X"
5212	            causal_match = re.search(
5213	                r'^(.*?)\s+(?:because|due to|resulting from)\s+(.+)$',
5214	                working, re.IGNORECASE,
5215	            )
5216	            if causal_match:
5217	                result_clause = causal_match.group(1).strip()
5218	                cause_clause = causal_match.group(2).strip()
5219	                # Capitalize cause, lowercase result
5220	                if cause_clause and cause_clause[0].islower():
5221	                    cause_clause = (
5222	                        cause_clause[0].upper() + cause_clause[1:]
5223	                    )
5224	                if result_clause and result_clause[0].isupper():
5225	                    result_clause = (
5226	                        result_clause[0].lower() + result_clause[1:]
5227	                    )
5228	                # Remove terminal punctuation from cause for joining
5229	                cause_clause = cause_clause.rstrip(".,;:")
5230	                rewritten = (
5231	                    f"{cause_clause}, leading to {result_clause}"
5232	                )
5233	                if rewritten and rewritten[-1] not in ".!?":
5234	                    rewritten += "."
5235	        else:
5236	            # Transform E: Active-to-passive voice rewrite (R7)
5237	            # "The study shows significant improvement" →
5238	            # "Significant improvement is shown by the study"
5239	            #
5240	            # Uses verb whitelist to avoid misidentifying nouns/
5241	            # adjectives as verbs. Only fires when the captured
5242	            # word is a known transitive verb.
5243	            active_match = re.match(
5244	                r'^((?:The|A|An|This|These|Each|Every)\s+'
5245	                r'(?:\w+\s+)*?\w+)'
5246	                r'\s+(\w+s)\s+(.+?)([.!?])$',
5247	                working, re.IGNORECASE,
5248	            )
5249	            passive_applied = False
5250	            if active_match:
5251	                subject = active_match.group(1).strip()
5252	                verb_raw = active_match.group(2).strip()
5253	                obj_phrase = active_match.group(3).strip()
5254	                terminal = active_match.group(4)
5255	
5256	                # Validate: verb must be a known transitive verb
5257	                verb_lower = verb_raw.lower()
5258	                verb_stem = verb_lower.rstrip("s")
5259	                # Also handle -es: "produces" → "produce"
5260	                if verb_stem.endswith("e") and verb_lower.endswith(
5261	                    "es",
5262	                ):
5263	                    verb_stem_alt = verb_stem
5264	                else:
5265	                    verb_stem_alt = None
5266	
5267	                if verb_stem in _R7_TRANSITIVE_VERBS or (
5268	                    verb_stem_alt
5269	                    and verb_stem_alt in _R7_TRANSITIVE_VERBS
5270	                ):
5271	                    # Derive past participle
5272	                    lookup = verb_stem
5273	                    if (
5274	                        lookup not in _R7_IRREGULAR_PP
5275	                        and verb_stem_alt
5276	                        and verb_stem_alt in _R7_IRREGULAR_PP
5277	                    ):
5278	                        lookup = verb_stem_alt
5279	                    if lookup in _R7_IRREGULAR_PP:
5280	                        pp = _R7_IRREGULAR_PP[lookup]
5281	                    elif lookup.endswith("e"):
5282	                        pp = lookup + "d"
5283	                    else:
5284	                        pp = lookup + "ed"
5285	
5286	                    # Capitalize object, lowercase subject
5287	                    if obj_phrase and obj_phrase[0].islower():
5288	                        obj_phrase = (
5289	                            obj_phrase[0].upper()
5290	                            + obj_phrase[1:]
5291	                        )
5292	                    subj_lower = subject
5293	                    if subj_lower and subj_lower[0].isupper():
5294	                        subj_lower = (
5295	                            subj_lower[0].lower()
5296	                            + subj_lower[1:]
5297	                        )
5298	
5299	                    # WS-2 Fix A: Extract trailing adverbs from
5300	                    # object phrase before building passive.
5301	                    # "harmful pollutants effectively" →
5302	                    # adverb "effectively" repositioned after aux.
5303	                    obj_words = obj_phrase.rstrip(
5304	                        ".,;:!? ",
5305	                    ).split()
5306	                    trailing_advs: list[str] = []
5307	                    while (
5308	                        obj_words
5309	                        and obj_words[-1].lower().endswith("ly")
5310	                        and len(obj_words[-1]) > 3
5311	                        and obj_words[-1].lower()
5312	                        not in _R7_SINGULAR_S
5313	                    ):
5314	                        trailing_advs.insert(0, obj_words.pop())
5315	                    obj_phrase_clean = " ".join(obj_words)
5316	                    if not obj_phrase_clean:
5317	                        obj_phrase_clean = obj_phrase.rstrip(
5318	                            ".,;:!? ",
5319	                        )
5320	                        trailing_advs = []
5321	                    adv_str = (
5322	                        " " + " ".join(trailing_advs)
5323	                        if trailing_advs else ""
5324	                    )
5325	
5326	                    # WS-2 Fix B: Plural check on cleaned phrase.
5327	                    # After extracting trailing adverbs, the LAST
5328	                    # word of the clean phrase is the grammatical
5329	                    # head (original heuristic, now safe from
5330	                    # adverb interference).
5331	                    aux = "is"
5332	                    clean_words = obj_phrase_clean.split()
5333	                    if clean_words:
5334	                        last_w = clean_words[-1].lower().rstrip(
5335	                            ".,;:!? ",
5336	                        )
5337	                        if (
5338	                            last_w.endswith("s")
5339	                            and last_w not in _R7_SINGULAR_S
5340	                        ):
5341	                            aux = "are"
5342	
5343	                    rewritten = (
5344	                        f"{obj_phrase_clean} {aux}{adv_str} "
5345	                        f"{pp} by {subj_lower}{terminal}"
5346	                    )
5347	                    passive_applied = True
5348	
5349	            if not passive_applied:
5350	                # Fallback: try to extract any number and foreground
5351	                # WP-1.1: Gated same as primary Transform B
5352	                if _transform_b:
5353	                    any_num = re.search(
5354	                        r'(\d+\.?\d*)\s*'
5355	                        r'(%|mg|ppt|ppb|ppm|µm|nm|\$|kWh|MPa|°C)',
5356	                        working,
5357	                    )
5358	                else:
5359	                    any_num = None
5360	                if any_num:
5361	                    number = any_num.group(1)
5362	                    unit = any_num.group(2)
5363	                    rest = working.replace(
5364	                        any_num.group(0), "", 1,
5365	                    ).strip()
5366	                    rest = re.sub(r'^(?:,\s*|\s+)', '', rest)
5367	                    if rest and rest[0].isupper():
5368	                        rest = rest[0].lower() + rest[1:]
5369	                    rewritten = f"With {number} {unit}, {rest}"
5370	                    if rewritten and rewritten[-1] not in ".!?":
5371	                        rewritten += "."
5372	
5373	        # Step 3: Restore citations from placeholders
5374	        for placeholder, cite in placeholders:
5375	            rewritten = rewritten.replace(placeholder, cite)
5376	
5377	        return rewritten

5379	    @staticmethod
5380	    def _synonym_rewrite(sentence: str, max_swaps: int = 3) -> str:
5381	        """P1: Synonym substitution for parroting mitigation.
5382	
5383	        BloomScrub pattern (arxiv:2504.16046): replace non-technical
5384	        connectors/adverbs with synonyms to reduce embedding similarity
5385	        while preserving domain meaning.
5386	
5387	        CR2: Only non-technical terms are swapped. Domain terms
5388	        (removal, concentration, treatment, etc.) are never changed.
5389	        """
5390	        # Skip verbatim-required sentences (patents, dollar figures, etc.)
5391	        if _VERBATIM_REQUIRED.search(sentence):
5392	            return sentence
5393	
5394	        # Step 1: Protect citations with placeholders
5395	        cite_re = re.compile(r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]')
5396	        cites = cite_re.findall(sentence)
5397	        working = sentence
5398	        cite_placeholders = []
5399	        for i, cite in enumerate(cites):
5400	            placeholder = f"__SYN_CITE_{i}__"
5401	            cite_placeholders.append((placeholder, cite))
5402	            working = working.replace(cite, placeholder, 1)
5403	
5404	        # Step 2: Apply synonym substitutions (case-preserving)
5405	        swaps = 0
5406	        words = working.split()
5407	        for wi, word in enumerate(words):
5408	            if swaps >= max_swaps:
5409	                break
5410	            # Strip punctuation for lookup
5411	            clean = word.strip(".,;:!?()[]\"'")
5412	            lower = clean.lower()
5413	            if lower in _SYNONYM_TABLE:
5414	                replacement = _SYNONYM_TABLE[lower]
5415	                # Preserve capitalization
5416	                if clean[0].isupper():
5417	                    replacement = replacement[0].upper() + replacement[1:]
5418	                # Preserve surrounding punctuation
5419	                words[wi] = word.replace(clean, replacement, 1)
5420	                swaps += 1
5421	
5422	        if swaps == 0:
5423	            return sentence
5424	
5425	        result = " ".join(words)
5426	
5427	        # Step 3: Restore citations
5428	        for placeholder, cite in cite_placeholders:
5429	            result = result.replace(placeholder, cite)
5430	
5431	        return result
5432	
5433	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5434	    # Generate-Then-Attribute: programmatic citation placement
5435	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5436	
5437	    def _attribute_citations(
5438	        self,
5439	        text: str,
5440	        threshold_override: float | None = None,
5441	    ) -> str:
5442	        """Add citations at sentence boundaries using 3-strategy matching.
5443	
5444	        Generate-Then-Attribute pattern (ACL 2024-2026): LLM writes clean
5445	        prose, citations placed programmatically using number matching,
5446	        keyword overlap, and embedding similarity.
5447	
5448	        Strategies (descending priority):
5449	        1. Number match: sentence number matches evidence number
5450	        2. Keyword overlap: 3+ content words shared with evidence
5451	        3. Embedding similarity: cosine sim >= threshold (fallback)
5452	
5453	        Citations placed at sentence boundary (before period).
5454	        Skips non-prose lines: tables, images, code blocks, headers.
5455	
5456	        Args:
5457	            text: Citation-free prose from LLM.
5458	            threshold_override: Override embedding threshold (for
5459	                quality gate re-attribution at lower threshold).
5460	
5461	        Returns:
5462	            Text with [CITE:ev_xxx] at sentence boundaries.
5463	        """
5464	        if not self._evidence_store:
5465	            return text
5466	
5467	        sim_threshold = (
5468	            threshold_override
5469	            if threshold_override is not None
5470	            else _GTA_THRESHOLD
5471	        )
5472	        max_per_sent = _GTA_MAX_PER_SENTENCE
5473	        keyword_min = _GTA_KEYWORD_MIN
5474	
5475	        # Build evidence lookup structures
5476	        all_eids = list(self._evidence_store.keys())
5477	        all_stmts = [
5478	            self._evidence_store[eid].get("statement", "")
5479	            for eid in all_eids
5480	        ]
5481	
5482	        # Pre-extract numbers from each evidence (filter trivial 0-9)
5483	        ev_numbers: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
5484	        for eid, stmt in zip(all_eids, all_stmts):
5485	            ev_numbers[eid] = {
5486	                n for n in re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*', stmt)
5487	                if len(n) >= 2 or float(n) >= 10
5488	            }
5489	
5490	        # Pre-extract content words from each evidence
5491	        _stopwords = frozenset({
5492	            "the", "and", "for", "with", "from", "that", "this",
5493	            "was", "were", "are", "been", "have", "has", "had",
5494	            "not", "but", "which", "their", "they", "than",
5495	            "can", "its", "also", "into", "more", "such",
5496	            "about", "through", "between", "after", "before",
5497	            "would", "could", "should", "will", "does", "did",
5498	            "being", "each", "other", "some", "what", "when",
5499	            "where", "while",
5500	        })
5501	        ev_words: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
5502	        for eid, stmt in zip(all_eids, all_stmts):
5503	            ev_words[eid] = {
5504	                w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', stmt.lower())
5505	                if w not in _stopwords
5506	            }
5507	
5508	        # Build embedding matrix lazily (only if needed)
5509	        _ev_emb_matrix = None
5510	        _ev_emb_norms = None
5511	
5512	        def _get_ev_embeddings():
5513	            nonlocal _ev_emb_matrix, _ev_emb_norms
5514	            if _ev_emb_matrix is None:
5515	                try:
5516	                    embs = embed_texts(all_stmts)
5517	                    _ev_emb_matrix = np.array(embs)
5518	                    norms = np.linalg.norm(
5519	                        _ev_emb_matrix, axis=1, keepdims=True,
5520	                    )
5521	                    _ev_emb_norms = _ev_emb_matrix / np.maximum(
5522	                        norms, 1e-8,
5523	                    )
5524	                except Exception:
5525	                    logger.debug(
5526	                        "[GTA] embed_texts failed for evidence",
5527	                        exc_info=True,
5528	                    )
5529	                    _ev_emb_matrix = np.zeros((0, 0))
5530	                    _ev_emb_norms = np.zeros((0, 0))
5531	            return _ev_emb_norms
5532	
5533	        # Process text line by line
5534	        output_lines = []
5535	        in_code_block = False
5536	        total_cites_added = 0
5537	
5538	        for line in text.split("\n"):
5539	            stripped = line.strip()
5540	
5541	            # Toggle code block state
5542	            if stripped.startswith("```"):
5543	                in_code_block = not in_code_block
5544	                output_lines.append(line)
5545	                continue
5546	
5547	            # Skip non-prose lines (CRITICAL-6 fix)
5548	            if (
5549	                in_code_block
5550	                or stripped.startswith("|")       # tables
5551	                or stripped.startswith("!")        # images
5552	                or stripped.startswith("#")        # headers
5553	                or stripped.startswith("-")        # list items
5554	                or stripped.startswith("*")        # list items
5555	                or not stripped                    # empty
5556	            ):
5557	                output_lines.append(line)
5558	                continue
5559	
5560	            # Split line into sentences
5561	            # Match sentence-ending punctuation while preserving
5562	            # the structure for reassembly
5563	            parts = re.split(r'([.!?](?:\s|$))', line)
5564	
5565	            rebuilt = ""
5566	            i = 0
5567	            while i < len(parts):
5568	                fragment = parts[i]
5569	                # Check if next part is the delimiter
5570	                delim = parts[i + 1] if i + 1 < len(parts) else ""
5571	                sentence = fragment + delim
5572	
5573	                if len(fragment.split()) < 3:
5574	                    # Too short to be a meaningful sentence
5575	                    rebuilt += sentence
5576	                    i += 2 if delim else 1
5577	                    continue
5578	
5579	                # Find matching evidence for this sentence
5580	                matched_eids: list[str] = []
5581	                sent_lower = fragment.lower()
5582	
5583	                # Strategy 1: Number matching (highest priority)
5584	                # Filter out trivially common numbers (0-9)
5585	                sent_nums = {
5586	                    n for n in re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*', fragment)
5587	                    if len(n) >= 2 or float(n) >= 10
5588	                }
5589	                if sent_nums:
5590	                    for eid in all_eids:
5591	                        shared = sent_nums & ev_numbers[eid]
5592	                        if shared and eid not in matched_eids:
5593	                            matched_eids.append(eid)
5594	                            if len(matched_eids) >= max_per_sent:
5595	                                break
5596	
5597	                # Strategy 2: Keyword overlap
5598	                if len(matched_eids) < max_per_sent:
5599	                    sent_words = {
5600	                        w for w in re.findall(
5601	                            r'[a-z]{4,}', sent_lower,
5602	                        )
5603	                        if w not in _stopwords
5604	                    }
5605	                    if sent_words:
5606	                        kw_candidates: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
5607	                        for eid in all_eids:
5608	                            if eid in matched_eids:
5609	                                continue
5610	                            overlap = len(sent_words & ev_words[eid])
5611	                            if overlap >= keyword_min:
5612	                                kw_candidates.append((overlap, eid))
5613	                        # Sort by overlap descending
5614	                        kw_candidates.sort(reverse=True)
5615	                        for _, eid in kw_candidates:
5616	                            if eid not in matched_eids:
5617	                                matched_eids.append(eid)
5618	                                if len(matched_eids) >= max_per_sent:
5619	                                    break
5620	
5621	                # Strategy 3: Embedding similarity (fallback)
5622	                if (
5623	                    len(matched_eids) < max_per_sent
5624	                    and len(fragment.split()) >= 5
5625	                ):
5626	                    ev_normed = _get_ev_embeddings()
5627	                    if ev_normed.size > 0:
5628	                        try:
5629	                            sent_emb = np.array(
5630	                                embed_texts([fragment]),
5631	                            )
5632	                            sent_norm = np.linalg.norm(
5633	                                sent_emb, axis=1, keepdims=True,
5634	                            )
5635	                            sent_normed = sent_emb / np.maximum(
5636	                                sent_norm, 1e-8,
5637	                            )
5638	                            sims = (sent_normed @ ev_normed.T)[0]
5639	                            top_indices = np.argsort(sims)[::-1]
5640	                            for si in top_indices:
5641	                                if sims[si] < sim_threshold:
5642	                                    break
5643	                                eid = all_eids[si]
5644	                                if eid not in matched_eids:
5645	                                    matched_eids.append(eid)
5646	                                    if (
5647	                                        len(matched_eids)
5648	                                        >= max_per_sent
5649	                                    ):
5650	                                        break
5651	                        except Exception:
5652	                            logger.debug(
5653	                                "[GTA] Embedding match failed "
5654	                                "for sentence",
5655	                                exc_info=True,
5656	                            )
5657	
5658	                # Place citations at sentence boundary
5659	                if matched_eids and delim:
5660	                    cite_str = "".join(
5661	                        f"[CITE:{eid}]"
5662	                        for eid in matched_eids[:max_per_sent]
5663	                    )
5664	                    # Insert before the period/punctuation
5665	                    rebuilt += fragment.rstrip() + " " + cite_str
5666	                    rebuilt += delim
5667	                    total_cites_added += len(
5668	                        matched_eids[:max_per_sent],
5669	                    )
5670	                else:
5671	                    rebuilt += sentence
5672	
5673	                i += 2 if delim else 1
5674	
5675	            output_lines.append(rebuilt)
5676	
5677	        result = "\n".join(output_lines)
5678	        logger.info(
5679	            "[GTA] Attribution complete: %d citations added "
5680	            "(threshold=%.2f)",
5681	            total_cites_added, sim_threshold,
5682	        )
5683	        return result

5685	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5686	    # WS-5: CiteFix citation correction (ACL 2025 Industry)
5687	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5688	
5689	    def _fix_citations(self, text: str) -> str:
5690	        """Correct misattributed citations using 3 strategies.
5691	
5692	        ACL 2025 finding: 80% of "hallucinations" in RAG are incorrect
5693	        citations, not fabricated facts. This method verifies each
5694	        citation against its surrounding context and swaps to the best
5695	        matching evidence when the original is wrong.
5696	
5697	        Strategies (sequential, first match wins):
5698	        1. Keyword matching: 3+ content words shared in 2-sentence window
5699	        2. Semantic matching: embedding similarity > 0.50
5700	        3. Number matching: numbers near citation must appear in evidence
5701	
5702	        MODERATE-1 guard: P7 swaps are tracked and skipped to avoid
5703	        undoing correct number-based fixes.
5704	        """
5705	        cite_pattern = re.compile(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]')
5706	        all_cites = list(cite_pattern.finditer(text))
5707	        if not all_cites:
5708	            return text
5709	
5710	        # Collect citation contexts: (eid, context_window, match_obj)
5711	        cite_contexts: list[tuple[str, str, re.Match]] = []
5712	        for cm in all_cites:
5713	            eid = cm.group(1)
5714	            if eid not in self._evidence_store:
5715	                continue
5716	            # 2-sentence window around citation
5717	            sent_start = text.rfind('.', 0, cm.start())
5718	            sent_start = sent_start + 1 if sent_start >= 0 else 0
5719	            # Look for 2 sentence ends after citation
5720	            first_end = text.find('.', cm.end())
5721	            if first_end >= 0:
5722	                second_end = text.find('.', first_end + 1)
5723	                sent_end = (
5724	                    second_end + 1 if second_end >= 0
5725	                    else first_end + 1
5726	                )
5727	            else:
5728	                sent_end = len(text)
5729	            ctx_window = text[sent_start:sent_end].strip()
5730	            if len(ctx_window) < 10:
5731	                continue
5732	            cite_contexts.append((eid, ctx_window, cm))
5733	
5734	        if not cite_contexts:
5735	            return text
5736	
5737	        # Strategy 1: Keyword matching
5738	        swaps: list[tuple[str, str]] = []  # (old_eid, new_eid)
5739	        unresolved_indices: list[int] = []
5740	        _stopwords = frozenset({
5741	            "the", "and", "for", "with", "from", "that", "this",
5742	            "was", "were", "are", "been", "have", "has", "had",
5743	            "not", "but", "which", "their", "they", "than",
5744	            "can", "its", "also", "into", "more", "such",
5745	        })
5746	
5747	        for idx, (eid, ctx, _cm) in enumerate(cite_contexts):
5748	            ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
5749	            ev_stmt = ev.get("statement", "").lower()
5750	            ctx_lower = ctx.lower()
5751	
5752	            # Extract content words (4+ chars, not stopwords)
5753	            ctx_words = {
5754	                w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ctx_lower)
5755	                if w not in _stopwords
5756	            }
5757	            ev_words = {
5758	                w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ev_stmt)
5759	                if w not in _stopwords
5760	            }
5761	            overlap = ctx_words & ev_words
5762	
5763	            if len(overlap) >= _CITEFIX_KEYWORD_MIN:
5764	                continue  # Citation matches — no fix needed
5765	
5766	            # Search all evidence for a better keyword match
5767	            best_eid = None
5768	            best_overlap = 0
5769	            for cand_eid, cand_ev in self._evidence_store.items():
5770	                if cand_eid == eid:
5771	                    continue
5772	                cand_stmt = cand_ev.get("statement", "").lower()
5773	                cand_words = {
5774	                    w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', cand_stmt)
5775	                    if w not in _stopwords
5776	                }
5777	                cand_overlap = len(ctx_words & cand_words)
5778	                if cand_overlap >= _CITEFIX_KEYWORD_MIN and (
5779	                    cand_overlap > best_overlap
5780	                ):
5781	                    best_eid = cand_eid
5782	                    best_overlap = cand_overlap
5783	
5784	            if best_eid:
5785	                swaps.append((eid, best_eid))
5786	            else:
5787	                unresolved_indices.append(idx)
5788	
5789	        # Strategy 2: Semantic matching for unresolved citations
5790	        if unresolved_indices and len(unresolved_indices) <= 50:
5791	            try:
5792	                unresolved_ctx = [
5793	                    cite_contexts[i][1] for i in unresolved_indices
5794	                ]
5795	                unresolved_eids = [
5796	                    cite_contexts[i][0] for i in unresolved_indices
5797	                ]
5798	                # Get all evidence statements
5799	                all_ev_ids = list(self._evidence_store.keys())
5800	                all_ev_stmts = [
5801	                    self._evidence_store[eid_k].get("statement", "")
5802	                    for eid_k in all_ev_ids
5803	                ]
5804	                # Batch embed
5805	                all_texts = unresolved_ctx + all_ev_stmts
5806	                all_embs = embed_texts(all_texts)
5807	                n_ctx = len(unresolved_ctx)
5808	                ctx_embs = np.array(all_embs[:n_ctx])
5809	                ev_embs = np.array(all_embs[n_ctx:])
5810	                # Cosine similarity matrix
5811	                ctx_norms = np.linalg.norm(ctx_embs, axis=1, keepdims=True)
5812	                ev_norms = np.linalg.norm(ev_embs, axis=1, keepdims=True)
5813	                ctx_normed = ctx_embs / np.maximum(ctx_norms, 1e-8)
5814	                ev_normed = ev_embs / np.maximum(ev_norms, 1e-8)
5815	                sim_matrix = ctx_normed @ ev_normed.T
5816	
5817	                still_unresolved: list[int] = []
5818	                for j, orig_idx in enumerate(unresolved_indices):
5819	                    orig_eid = unresolved_eids[j]
5820	                    sims = sim_matrix[j]
5821	                    # Find best match that isn't the current citation
5822	                    sorted_indices = np.argsort(sims)[::-1]
5823	                    for si in sorted_indices:
5824	                        cand_eid = all_ev_ids[si]
5825	                        if cand_eid == orig_eid:
5826	                            continue
5827	                        if sims[si] >= _CITEFIX_SEMANTIC_THRESHOLD:
5828	                            swaps.append((orig_eid, cand_eid))
5829	                            break
5830	                    else:
5831	                        still_unresolved.append(orig_idx)
5832	
5833	                # Strategy 3: Number matching for remaining
5834	                for orig_idx in still_unresolved:
5835	                    eid_3, ctx_3, _cm_3 = cite_contexts[orig_idx]
5836	                    ctx_nums = set(re.findall(
5837	                        r'\d+\.?\d*', ctx_3,
5838	                    ))
5839	                    if not ctx_nums:
5840	                        continue
5841	                    for cand_eid, cand_ev in self._evidence_store.items():
5842	                        if cand_eid == eid_3:
5843	                            continue
5844	                        cand_stmt = cand_ev.get("statement", "")
5845	                        cand_nums = set(re.findall(
5846	                            r'\d+\.?\d*', cand_stmt,
5847	                        ))
5848	                        shared_nums = ctx_nums & cand_nums
5849	                        if shared_nums:
5850	                            swaps.append((eid_3, cand_eid))
5851	                            break
5852	            except Exception:
5853	                logger.debug(
5854	                    "[CiteFix] Semantic/number matching failed",
5855	                    exc_info=True,
5856	                )
5857	
5858	        # Apply swaps (deduplicate: each citation swapped at most once)
5859	        if swaps:
5860	            applied = 0
5861	            seen_swaps: set[str] = set()
5862	            for old_eid, new_eid in swaps:
5863	                if old_eid in seen_swaps:
5864	                    continue
5865	                old_cite = f"[CITE:{old_eid}]"
5866	                new_cite = f"[CITE:{new_eid}]"
5867	                if old_cite in text:
5868	                    text = text.replace(old_cite, new_cite, 1)
5869	                    seen_swaps.add(old_eid)
5870	                    applied += 1
5871	                    logger.info(
5872	                        "[CiteFix] Swapped citation: %s -> %s",
5873	                        old_eid[:16], new_eid[:16],
5874	                    )
5875	            if applied > 0:
5876	                logger.info(
5877	                    "[CiteFix] Corrected %d citations (%d candidates)",
5878	                    applied, len(swaps),
5879	                )
5880	
5881	        return text

5884	    # Phase 6 (legacy): CRITIQUE — structured quality check
5885	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
5886	
5887	    async def _critique_interpretation(
5888	        self, interpretation: str, briefing: dict,
5889	    ) -> dict | None:
5890	        """Critique interpretation for analytical quality (Phase 6).
5891	
5892	        Uses reason() to evaluate against 5 substance dimensions.
5893	        Returns structured critique dict with pass/fail per dimension.
5894	        """
5895	        sub_questions = briefing.get("sub_questions", [])
5896	        sq_text = "\n".join(f"  - {q}" for q in sub_questions)
5897	
5898	        prompt = (
5899	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION: {self._query}\n\n"
5900	            f"SUB-QUESTIONS THE ANALYSIS SHOULD ADDRESS:\n{sq_text}\n\n"
5901	            f"ANALYSIS TO CRITIQUE:\n{interpretation}\n\n"
5902	            f"Evaluate this analysis on 5 dimensions. For each, state "
5903	            f"whether it PASSES or FAILS and list specific issues:\n\n"
5904	            f"1. sub_question_coverage: Does it address ALL sub-questions?\n"
5905	            f"2. cross_source_synthesis: Are there sentences combining "
5906	            f"2+ sources? (look for multiple [CITE:] in one sentence)\n"
5907	            f"3. integration: For multi-criteria queries, are criteria "
5908	            f"discussed TOGETHER (not in separate sections)?\n"
5909	            f"4. evidence_grounding: Does every numerical claim have a "
5910	            f"[CITE:ev_xxx]?\n"
5911	            f"5. analytical_depth: Are there trade-off identifications, "
5912	            f"conditional recommendations, gap analyses?\n\n"
5913	            f"Then decide: needs_rewrite = true if <=3 dimensions pass.\n"
5914	            f"If rewrite needed, provide specific fix instructions."
5915	        )
5916	
5917	        system = (
5918	            "You are a research quality auditor. Be strict but fair. "
5919	            "Return valid JSON matching the InterpretationCritique schema."
5920	        )
5921	
5922	        try:
5923	            response = await asyncio.wait_for(
5924	                self._client.reason(
5925	                    prompt=prompt,
5926	                    system=system,
5927	                    schema=InterpretationCritique,
5928	                    # I-arch-003 (#1253): reasoning effort to MAX (was medium). max_tokens routes through
5929	                    # openrouter_client so the reasoning-first floor (>=4096/16384) applies.
5930	                    effort=os.environ.get("PG_CRITIQUE_REASONING_EFFORT", "high") or "high",
5931	                    # F23 (I-arch-004 A3): env-overridable audit-critique cap; default keeps the
5932	                    # historical literal 2048 (byte-identical when unset). CAP not target (§9.1.8).
5933	                    max_tokens=int(os.environ.get("PG_AUDIT_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOKENS", "2048")),
5934	                    timeout=_CRITIQUE_TIMEOUT,
5935	                ),
5936	                timeout=_CRITIQUE_TIMEOUT + 15,
5937	            )
5938	
5939	            # Parse the response
5940	            content = response.content.strip()
5941	            if hasattr(response, "_parsed") and response._parsed:
5942	                critique_obj = response._parsed
5943	            else:
5944	                # Try to parse JSON from content
5945	                try:
5946	                    critique_obj = InterpretationCritique.model_validate_json(
5947	                        content,
5948	                    )
5949	                except Exception:
5950	                    # Try extracting JSON from content
5951	                    json_match = re.search(
5952	                        r'\{[\s\S]*\}', content,
5953	                    )
5954	                    if json_match:
5955	                        critique_obj = InterpretationCritique.model_validate_json(
5956	                            json_match.group(),
5957	                        )
5958	                    else:
5959	                        logger.warning(
5960	                            "[8phase] Could not parse critique response",
5961	                        )
5962	                        return self._programmatic_critique(
5963	                            interpretation, briefing,
5964	                        )
5965	
5966	            return critique_obj.model_dump()
5967	
5968	        except Exception as exc:
5969	            logger.warning(
5970	                "[8phase] Critique failed: %s: %s, using programmatic",
5971	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
5972	            )
5973	            return self._programmatic_critique(interpretation, briefing)
5974	
5975	    def _programmatic_critique(
5976	        self, interpretation: str, briefing: dict,
5977	    ) -> dict:
5978	        """Programmatic fallback critique when LLM critique fails.
5979	
5980	        Checks the 5 dimensions using regex and counting.
5981	        """
5982	        dims = []
5983	
5984	        # 1. Sub-question coverage
5985	        sub_questions = briefing.get("sub_questions", [])
5986	        covered = 0
5987	        for sq in sub_questions:
5988	            sq_words = set(
5989	                w for w in sq.lower().split() if len(w) > 3
5990	            )
5991	            interp_lower = interpretation.lower()
5992	            overlap = sum(1 for w in sq_words if w in interp_lower)
5993	            if overlap >= 2:
5994	                covered += 1
5995	        coverage_ratio = covered / max(len(sub_questions), 1)
5996	        dims.append({
5997	            "dimension": "sub_question_coverage",
5998	            "passed": coverage_ratio >= 0.6,
5999	            "issues": (

6000	                [f"Only {covered}/{len(sub_questions)} sub-questions addressed"]
6001	                if coverage_ratio < 0.6 else []
6002	            ),
6003	        })
6004	
6005	        # 2. Cross-source synthesis
6006	        cross_count = 0
6007	        for sentence in re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', interpretation):
6008	            cites = set(re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', sentence))
6009	            if len(cites) >= 2:
6010	                cross_count += 1
6011	        dims.append({
6012	            "dimension": "cross_source_synthesis",
6013	            "passed": cross_count >= 3,
6014	            "issues": (
6015	                [f"Only {cross_count} cross-source sentences (need >=3)"]
6016	                if cross_count < 3 else []
6017	            ),
6018	        })
6019	
6020	        # 3. Integration (multi-criteria in same paragraph)
6021	        paragraphs = [
6022	            p.strip() for p in interpretation.split("\n\n") if p.strip()
6023	        ]
6024	        criteria_words = {
6025	            "cost", "price", "expensive", "affordable",
6026	            "effective", "efficiency", "removal", "performance",
6027	        }
6028	        integrated_paragraphs = 0
6029	        for para in paragraphs:
6030	            para_lower = para.lower()
6031	            criteria_found = sum(
6032	                1 for w in criteria_words if w in para_lower
6033	            )
6034	            if criteria_found >= 3:
6035	                integrated_paragraphs += 1
6036	        integration_ratio = integrated_paragraphs / max(len(paragraphs), 1)
6037	        dims.append({
6038	            "dimension": "integration",
6039	            "passed": integration_ratio >= 0.2 or len(sub_questions) < 3,
6040	            "issues": (
6041	                [f"Only {integrated_paragraphs}/{len(paragraphs)} paragraphs "
6042	                 f"integrate multiple criteria"]
6043	                if integration_ratio < 0.2 and len(sub_questions) >= 3 else []
6044	            ),
6045	        })
6046	
6047	        # 4. Evidence grounding
6048	        # Count numerical claims without citations
6049	        num_claims = re.findall(
6050	            r'\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|mg|ng|ppt|ppb|ppm|kWh|\$)',
6051	            interpretation,
6052	        )
6053	        cited_nums = re.findall(
6054	            r'\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|mg|ng|ppt|ppb|ppm|kWh|\$)[^.!?\n]*'
6055	            r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]',
6056	            interpretation,
6057	        )
6058	        grounding_ratio = len(cited_nums) / max(len(num_claims), 1)
6059	        dims.append({
6060	            "dimension": "evidence_grounding",
6061	            "passed": grounding_ratio >= 0.6,
6062	            "issues": (
6063	                [f"Only {len(cited_nums)}/{len(num_claims)} numerical "
6064	                 f"claims have citations"]
6065	                if grounding_ratio < 0.6 else []
6066	            ),
6067	        })
6068	
6069	        # 5. Analytical depth
6070	        depth_markers = [
6071	            r'(?:however|although|while|whereas|despite)',
6072	            r'(?:trade-?off|limitation|disadvantage|drawback)',
6073	            r'(?:recommend|ranking|prefer|optimal|best suited)',
6074	            r'(?:gap|limitation|missing|insufficient|unclear)',
6075	        ]
6076	        depth_count = sum(
6077	            len(re.findall(p, interpretation, re.IGNORECASE))
6078	            for p in depth_markers
6079	        )
6080	        dims.append({
6081	            "dimension": "analytical_depth",
6082	            "passed": depth_count >= 3,
6083	            "issues": (
6084	                [f"Only {depth_count} depth markers found (need >=3)"]
6085	                if depth_count < 3 else []
6086	            ),
6087	        })
6088	
6089	        passed_count = sum(1 for d in dims if d["passed"])
6090	        needs_rewrite = passed_count <= 3
6091	
6092	        all_issues = []
6093	        for d in dims:
6094	            all_issues.extend(d["issues"])
6095	
6096	        return {
6097	            "dimensions": dims,
6098	            "needs_rewrite": needs_rewrite,
6099	            "rewrite_instructions": (
6100	                "Fix these issues: " + "; ".join(all_issues)
6101	                if needs_rewrite else ""
6102	            ),
6103	        }
6104	
6105	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6106	    # Phase 7: REWRITE — fix critique issues
6107	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6108	
6109	    async def _rewrite_interpretation(
6110	        self, interpretation: str, critique: dict, briefing: dict,
6111	    ) -> str | None:
6112	        """Rewrite interpretation to fix critique issues (Phase 7).
6113	
6114	        Only called when critique.needs_rewrite == True.
6115	        Returns rewritten text, or None if rewrite fails/is too short.
6116	        """
6117	        all_issues = []
6118	        for dim in critique.get("dimensions", []):
6119	            if not dim.get("passed", True):
6120	                all_issues.extend(dim.get("issues", []))
6121	
6122	        instructions = critique.get("rewrite_instructions", "")
6123	        issue_list = "\n".join(f"- {issue}" for issue in all_issues)
6124	
6125	        prompt = (
6126	            f"ORIGINAL ANALYSIS:\n{interpretation}\n\n"
6127	            f"CRITIQUE FINDINGS:\n{instructions}\n\n"
6128	            f"REWRITE the analysis to fix these specific issues:\n"
6129	            f"{issue_list}\n\n"
6130	            f"RULES:\n"
6131	            f"1. Preserve all existing CORRECT claims and citations\n"
6132	            f"2. Fix ONLY the issues identified above\n"
6133	            f"3. Do NOT shorten the analysis\n"
6134	            f"4. Maintain [CITE:ev_xxx] format for all citations\n"
6135	            f"5. Do NOT add claims without evidence\n"
6136	            f"6. Target 800-1500 words\n"
6137	        )
6138	
6139	        system = (
6140	            "Rewrite the analysis to fix the identified issues. "
6141	            "Preserve correct claims and citations. Do not shorten."
6142	        )
6143	
6144	        try:
6145	            response = await asyncio.wait_for(
6146	                self._client.generate(
6147	                    prompt=prompt,
6148	                    system=system,
6149	                    max_tokens=8192,
6150	                    temperature=0.3,
6151	                    timeout=_SCAFFOLD_TIMEOUT,
6152	                ),
6153	                timeout=_SCAFFOLD_TIMEOUT + 30,
6154	            )
6155	
6156	            rewritten = response.content.strip()
6157	
6158	            # Safety: accept only if >= 70% of original length
6159	            if len(rewritten) < 0.7 * len(interpretation):
6160	                logger.warning(
6161	                    "[8phase] Rewrite too short: %d vs %d (%.0f%%), keeping original",
6162	                    len(rewritten), len(interpretation),
6163	                    len(rewritten) / max(len(interpretation), 1) * 100,
6164	                )
6165	                return None
6166	
6167	            # Remove phantom citations
6168	            all_cited = re.findall(r'\[CITE:([^\]]+)\]', rewritten)
6169	            for pid in set(all_cited):
6170	                if pid not in self._evidence_store:
6171	                    rewritten = rewritten.replace(f"[CITE:{pid}]", "")
6172	
6173	            # Update the interpretation step in notebook
6174	            for step in self._notebook.steps:
6175	                if (
6176	                    step.tool_name == "interpret_results"
6177	                    and step.result.success
6178	                ):
6179	                    valid_ids = [
6180	                        eid for eid in re.findall(
6181	                            r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', rewritten,
6182	                        )
6183	                        if eid in self._evidence_store
6184	                    ]
6185	                    step.result = ToolResult(
6186	                        success=True,
6187	                        tool_name="interpret_results",
6188	                        markdown=rewritten,
6189	                        source_evidence_ids=list(set(valid_ids)),
6190	                        insights=[
6191	                            "Scaffold-based analysis (rewritten after critique)",
6192	                        ],
6193	                    )
6194	                    break
6195	
6196	            return rewritten
6197	
6198	        except Exception as exc:
6199	            logger.warning(
6200	                "[8phase] Rewrite failed: %s: %s",
6201	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
6202	            )
6203	            return None
6204	
6205	    async def _plan_analysis(self) -> AnalysisPlan:
6206	        """Plan analysis in ONE LLM call (ReWOO pattern).
6207	
6208	        Returns an ordered list of tools to execute. Falls back to
6209	        a deterministic plan [extract, stats, sql] on failure.
6210	        """
6211	        all_tools = self._registry.available_tools(has_data=True)
6212	        evidence_count = len(self._evidence_ids)
6213	        has_structured = any(
6214	            self._evidence_store.get(eid, {}).get("structured_data")
6215	            for eid in self._evidence_ids[:50]
6216	        )
6217	
6218	        prompt = (
6219	            f"Plan analysis for: {self._query[:120]}\n"
6220	            f"Evidence: {evidence_count} | Structured: {has_structured}\n"
6221	            f"Tools: {', '.join(all_tools)}\n\n"
6222	            f"Rules:\n"
6223	            f"1. Always start with extract_numeric_data\n"
6224	            f"2. Then statistical_summary or query_evidence_sql\n"
6225	            f"3. Then comparison_table or meta_analysis\n"
6226	            f"4. Max {_MAX_ITERATIONS} steps\n\n"
6227	            f"Return ordered tool steps."
6228	        )
6229	
6230	        # INF-2: Cap plan timeout at 30s (fallback handles failure)
6231	        plan_timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_PLAN_TIMEOUT", "30"))
6232	        plan = await asyncio.wait_for(
6233	            self._client.generate_structured(
6234	                prompt=prompt,
6235	                schema=AnalysisPlan,
6236	                system="Plan the analysis. Return an ordered list of tool steps.",
6237	                max_tokens=512,
6238	                timeout=plan_timeout,
6239	            ),
6240	            timeout=plan_timeout + 15,
6241	        )
6242	
6243	        # Filter to known tools only, cap at max iterations
6244	        valid_steps = [
6245	            step for step in plan.steps
6246	            if step.tool_name in _KNOWN_TOOLS and step.tool_name != "stop"
6247	        ]
6248	        plan.steps = valid_steps[:_MAX_ITERATIONS]
6249	
6250	        return plan
6251	
6252	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6253	    # Phase 6.25: Visual artifacts (VIZ-1, VIZ-2, VIZ-3)
6254	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6255	
6256	    async def _generate_charts(
6257	        self,
6258	        classification: dict | None,
6259	        briefing: dict,
6260	    ) -> str:
6261	        """VIZ-1: Auto-generate matplotlib chart via execute_python.
6262	
6263	        Only generates charts when data points have ≥3 entries with
6264	        same unit AND ≥2 distinct labels. Returns markdown image embed.
6265	        """
6266	        data_points = self._notebook.data_points
6267	        if not data_points:
6268	            return ""
6269	
6270	        archetype = (classification or {}).get("archetype", "general")
6271	        if archetype == "mechanism":
6272	            return ""  # Causal chains don't map to bar charts
6273	
6274	        # Check chart-worthiness: group by unit, need ≥3 points + ≥2 labels
6275	        by_unit: dict[str, list] = {}
6276	        for dp in data_points:
6277	            unit = dp.get("unit", "")
6278	            if unit:
6279	                by_unit.setdefault(unit, []).append(dp)
6280	
6281	        best_unit = ""
6282	        best_group = []
6283	        for unit, group in by_unit.items():
6284	            labels = set(dp.get("label", "") for dp in group)
6285	            if len(group) >= 3 and len(labels) >= 2:
6286	                if len(group) > len(best_group):
6287	                    best_unit = unit
6288	                    best_group = group
6289	
6290	        if not best_group:
6291	            return ""
6292	
6293	        # Determine chart type
6294	        chart_type = "barh"  # Default horizontal bar
6295	        if archetype == "cost_analysis":
6296	            chart_type = "bar"  # Grouped vertical bar
6297	
6298	        # FIX-CRASH: Build data for the chart — type-safe via _safe_float
6299	        labels = []
6300	        values = []
6301	        eids = []
6302	        for dp in best_group[:15]:  # Cap at 15 bars
6303	            label = dp.get("label", "unknown")[:30]
6304	            float_val = _safe_float(dp.get("value"))
6305	            eid = dp.get("evidence_id", "")
6306	            if float_val is not None and label:
6307	                labels.append(label)
6308	                values.append(float_val)
6309	                eids.append(eid)
6310	
6311	        if len(labels) < 2:
6312	            return ""
6313	
6314	        # Generate chart via execute_python tool
6315	        metric_name = best_group[0].get("metric", "Value")
6316	        tool_def = self._registry.get_tool("execute_python")
6317	        if not tool_def or not tool_def.execute:
6318	            return ""
6319	
6320	        chart_timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_CHART_TIMEOUT", "60"))
6321	        try:
6322	            result = await asyncio.wait_for(
6323	                tool_def.execute(
6324	                    evidence_store=self._evidence_store,
6325	                    data_points=data_points,
6326	                    client=self._client,
6327	                    question=(
6328	                        f"Create a {'horizontal ' if chart_type == 'barh' else ''}"
6329	                        f"bar chart comparing: "
6330	                        f"labels={labels}, values={values}, "
6331	                        f"unit='{best_unit}', metric='{metric_name}'. "
6332	                        f"Use clear colors, annotate bars with values. "
6333	                        f"Title: '{metric_name} by Entity'. "
6334	                        f"Return as PNG."
6335	                    ),
6336	                    research_context=self._query,
6337	                ),
6338	                timeout=chart_timeout,
6339	            )
6340	        except Exception as exc:
6341	            logger.warning(
6342	                "[chart] execute_python failed: %s: %s",
6343	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:100],
6344	            )
6345	            return ""
6346	
6347	        if not result.success or not result.charts:
6348	            return ""
6349	
6350	        # VIZ-2: Embed chart as base64 image reference
6351	        chart = result.charts[0]
6352	        b64 = chart.get("image_base64", "")
6353	        if not b64:
6354	            return ""
6355	
6356	        chart_title = f"{metric_name} Comparison ({best_unit})"
6357	        step = AnalysisStep(
6358	            step_number=self._notebook.step_count + 1,
6359	            reasoning=f"Auto-generated {chart_type} chart: {chart_title}",
6360	            tool_name="auto_chart",
6361	            result=ToolResult(
6362	                success=True,
6363	                tool_name="auto_chart",
6364	                markdown=f"![{chart_title}](chart)",
6365	                source_evidence_ids=eids[:10],
6366	                charts=[chart],
6367	                insights=[f"Chart: {chart_title}"],
6368	            ),
6369	            elapsed_seconds=0.0,
6370	        )
6371	        self._notebook.add_step(step)
6372	
6373	        logger.info(
6374	            "[chart] Generated %s chart: %d bars, %s",
6375	            chart_type, len(labels), chart_title,
6376	        )
6377	        return f"\n![{chart_title}](data:image/png;base64,{b64})\n"
6378	
6379	    def _generate_decision_flowchart(self, text: str) -> str:
6380	        """VIZ-3: Text-based decision tree from conditional recs.
6381	
6382	        Only generated when ≥2 **If** ... **then** patterns exist.
6383	        Pure text, no Mermaid dependency, renders everywhere.
6384	        """
6385	        # Find conditional recommendations in the text
6386	        if_then_pattern = re.compile(
6387	            r'\*\*[Ii]f\*\*\s*(.{10,120}?)\s*\*\*then\*\*\s*'
6388	            r'(.{5,80}?)\s*(?:\*\*because\*\*\s*)?'
6389	            r'(.{0,120}?)(?:\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\])?'
6390	            r'(?:\.|$)',
6391	            re.DOTALL,
6392	        )
6393	        matches = list(if_then_pattern.finditer(text))
6394	
6395	        if len(matches) < 2:
6396	            return ""
6397	
6398	        lines = [
6399	            "### Decision Guide\n",
6400	            "```",
6401	        ]
6402	
6403	        for i, match in enumerate(matches[:5]):
6404	            condition = match.group(1).strip().rstrip(",")[:60]
6405	            recommendation = match.group(2).strip().rstrip(",")[:40]
6406	            eid = match.group(4) or ""
6407	            cite = f" [CITE:{eid}]" if eid else ""
6408	
6409	            prefix = "├─" if i < len(matches) - 1 else "└─"
6410	            lines.append(
6411	                f"  {prefix} {condition}? → {recommendation}{cite}"
6412	            )
6413	
6414	        lines.append("```")
6415	
6416	        logger.info(
6417	            "[decision-tree] Generated from %d conditional recs",
6418	            len(matches),
6419	        )
6420	        return "\n".join(lines)
6421	
6422	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6423	    # Post-processing: cleanup LLM output defects
6424	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
6425	
6426	    def _post_process_interpretation(self, text: str) -> str:
6427	        """Clean up common LLM output defects.
6428	
6429	        1. Remove duplicate sentences (DeRep pattern: cosine > 0.95)
6430	        2. Strip meta-commentary about prompt rules/constraints
6431	        3. Flag fabricated numbers not in any cited evidence
6432	        """
6433	        # D3: Normalize line endings (handles \r\n from Windows/mixed)
6434	        text = text.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n")
6435	
6436	        # D1-FIX: Multi-layer CoT scrubber (must run FIRST, before any
6437	        # line-based processing). Qwen 3.5 Plus via OpenRouter occasionally
6438	        # leaks </think> tags into the content field.
6439	        # Layer 1: Complete <think>...</think> blocks
6440	        text = re.sub(
6441	            r'<think>.*?</think>', '', text,
6442	            flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE,
6443	        )
6444	        # Layer 2: Everything before orphan </think> (preamble leak)
6445	        text = re.sub(
6446	            r'^.*?</think>\s*', '', text,
6447	            flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE,
6448	        )
6449	        # Layer 3: Orphan open/close tags
6450	        text = re.sub(r'</?think>', '', text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
6451	
6452	        lines = text.split("\n")
6453	        cleaned_lines = []
6454	
6455	        # --- Fix 1: Remove duplicate sentences within each paragraph ---
6456	        seen_sentences: set[str] = set()
6457	        for line in lines:
6458	            if not line.strip():
6459	                cleaned_lines.append(line)
6460	                continue
6461	
6462	            sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', line)
6463	            unique_sentences = []
6464	            for sent in sentences:
6465	                # Normalize for comparison: lowercase, strip citations
6466	                norm = re.sub(
6467	                    r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]', '', sent,
6468	                ).strip().lower()
6469	                # R4: Short sentences (<25 chars or <5 words) are
6470	                # exempt from dedup to protect domain phrases like
6471	                # "GAC is effective". Long sentences always dedup.
6472	                norm_words = norm.split()
6473	                if len(norm) < 25 or len(norm_words) < 5:
6474	                    unique_sentences.append(sent)
6475	                    continue
6476	                if norm not in seen_sentences:
6477	                    seen_sentences.add(norm)
6478	                    unique_sentences.append(sent)
6479	                else:
6480	                    logger.debug(
6481	                        "[post-process] Removed duplicate: %s",
6482	                        sent[:60],
6483	                    )
6484	
6485	            if unique_sentences:
6486	                cleaned_lines.append(" ".join(unique_sentences))
6487	
6488	        text = "\n".join(cleaned_lines)
6489	
6490	        # --- Fix 1c: Remove intra-sentence redundancy ---
6491	        # "superior uniformity...through processes that yield superior
6492	        # uniformity" — find 4+ word phrases appearing twice, remove
6493	        # the second occurrence with its connecting clause.
6494	        new_lines = []
6495	        for line in text.split("\n"):
6496	            sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', line)
6497	            deduped_sents = []
6498	            for sent in sentences:
6499	                words = sent.lower().split()
6500	                if len(words) < 10:
6501	                    deduped_sents.append(sent)
6502	                    continue
6503	                # Find any repeated multi-word phrase (>=8 chars)
6504	                # in the sentence and remove the second clause
6505	                # containing it
6506	                cleaned = sent
6507	                sent_lower = sent.lower()
6508	                for phrase_len in range(6, 1, -1):
6509	                    for i in range(len(words) - phrase_len):
6510	                        phrase = " ".join(words[i:i + phrase_len])
6511	                        if len(phrase) < 15:
6512	                            continue
6513	                        first_pos = sent_lower.find(phrase)
6514	                        if first_pos < 0:
6515	                            continue
6516	                        second_pos = sent_lower.find(
6517	                            phrase, first_pos + len(phrase),
6518	                        )
6519	                        if second_pos < 0:
6520	                            continue
6521	                        # Found duplicate phrase — remove the
6522	                        # second occurrence with connectors
6523	                        # Work on the original-case text
6524	                        before = cleaned[:second_pos]
6525	                        after = cleaned[second_pos + len(phrase):]
6526	                        # Strip trailing connector before the dup
6527	                        before = re.sub(
6528	                            r'[,;]?\s*(?:through\s+)?'
6529	                            r'(?:processes\s+that\s+)?'
6530	                            r'(?:that\s+)?(?:which\s+)?'
6531	                            r'(?:yielding\s+)?(?:yield\s+)?$',
6532	                            '', before,
6533	                        )
6534	                        cleaned = before + after
6535	                        logger.debug(
6536	                            "[post-process] Removed intra-sentence "
6537	                            "repeat: '%s'", phrase[:40],
6538	                        )
6539	                        break
6540	                    else:
6541	                        continue
6542	                    break
6543	                deduped_sents.append(cleaned)
6544	            new_lines.append(" ".join(deduped_sents))
6545	        text = "\n".join(new_lines)
6546	
6547	        # --- Fix 1d: FIX-D5 — Near-duplicate sentence detection ---
6548	        # Within each section (split by ### headings), compute word-level
6549	        # 5-gram Jaccard between sentence pairs. If > 0.70, keep the
6550	        # longer/more-cited sentence.
6551	        # IMPORTANT: Process line-by-line to preserve paragraph breaks
6552	        # and blank lines. Compare across lines within the same section.
6553	        sections = re.split(r'(^#{2,4}\s+.+$)', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
6554	        rebuilt_sections = []
6555	
6556	        def _word_5grams(s: str) -> set:
6557	            words = re.findall(r'[a-z]{3,}', s.lower())
6558	            return {
6559	                tuple(words[i:i + 5])
6560	                for i in range(len(words) - 4)
6561	            } if len(words) >= 5 else set()
6562	
6563	        for section_chunk in sections:
6564	            if not section_chunk.strip() or re.match(
6565	                r'^#{2,4}\s+', section_chunk,
6566	            ):
6567	                rebuilt_sections.append(section_chunk)
6568	                continue
6569	
6570	            # Collect all sentences across lines, tracking origin
6571	            sec_lines = section_chunk.split("\n")
6572	            all_sents: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
6573	            for li, line in enumerate(sec_lines):
6574	                if not line.strip():
6575	                    continue
6576	                sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', line)
6577	                for si, sent in enumerate(sents):
6578	                    all_sents.append((li, si, sent))
6579	
6580	            if len(all_sents) < 2:
6581	                rebuilt_sections.append(section_chunk)
6582	                continue
6583	
6584	            # Build 5-gram sets
6585	            gram_cache = {
6586	                idx: _word_5grams(s) for idx, (_, _, s)
6587	                in enumerate(all_sents)
6588	            }
6589	
6590	            # Mark sentences to remove (by their index in all_sents)
6591	            to_remove: set[int] = set()
6592	            for i in range(len(all_sents)):
6593	                if i in to_remove:
6594	                    continue
6595	                g_i = gram_cache.get(i, set())
6596	                if not g_i:
6597	                    continue
6598	                for j in range(i + 1, len(all_sents)):
6599	                    if j in to_remove:
6600	                        continue
6601	                    g_j = gram_cache.get(j, set())
6602	                    if not g_j:
6603	                        continue
6604	                    overlap = len(g_i & g_j)
6605	                    union = len(g_i | g_j)
6606	                    if union > 0 and overlap / union > 0.70:
6607	                        si = all_sents[i][2]
6608	                        sj = all_sents[j][2]
6609	                        cite_i = len(re.findall(r'\[CITE:', si))
6610	                        cite_j = len(re.findall(r'\[CITE:', sj))
6611	                        if (
6612	                            len(sj) > len(si)
6613	                            or (len(sj) == len(si) and cite_j > cite_i)
6614	                        ):
6615	                            to_remove.add(i)
6616	                            logger.debug(
6617	                                "[post-process] FIX-D5: Near-dup "
6618	                                "removed (Jaccard=%.2f): %s",
6619	                                overlap / union, si[:60],
6620	                            )
6621	                            break
6622	                        else:
6623	                            to_remove.add(j)
6624	                            logger.debug(
6625	                                "[post-process] FIX-D5: Near-dup "
6626	                                "removed (Jaccard=%.2f): %s",

7603	        # --- TQ-1: Table cell verbosity trimming ---
7604	        # Cells >60 chars: keep number+unit+qualifier (≤30 chars)
7605	        def _trim_cell(cell_text: str) -> str:
7606	            if len(cell_text) <= 60:
7607	                return cell_text
7608	            # Preserve citations
7609	            cites = re.findall(r'\[CITE:ev_[a-f0-9]+\]', cell_text)
7610	            cite_str = " ".join(cites)
7611	            # Extract first number + unit + qualifier (up to 30 chars)
7612	            num_match = re.search(
7613	                r'(\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|mg|ng|ppt|ppb|ppm|kWh|\$|MPa|'
7614	                r'µm|nm|m2|g/L|mg/g|billion|million|°C|bar|min|h|'
7615	                r'L|mL|kg)(?:\s*[^\d\[]{0,25})?)',
7616	                cell_text,
7617	            )
7618	            if num_match:
7619	                core = num_match.group(1).strip()[:30]
7620	                return f"{core} {cite_str}".strip()
7621	            return cell_text[:30] + f"... {cite_str}".strip()

7623	        table_line_pattern = re.compile(r'^\|(.+)\|$', re.MULTILINE)
7624	        sep_pattern = re.compile(r'^\|[-:| ]+\|$', re.MULTILINE)
7625	        new_lines = []
7626	        for line in text.split("\n"):
7627	            if (
7628	                table_line_pattern.match(line.strip())
7629	                and not sep_pattern.match(line.strip())
7630	            ):
7631	                cells = line.split("|")

7635	                        trimmed.append(f" {_trim_cell(cell.strip())} ")
7636	                    else:
7637	                        trimmed.append(cell)
7638	                new_lines.append("|".join(trimmed))
7639	            else:
7640	                new_lines.append(line)

7643	        # --- WP-1.4: Normalize citation token whitespace before dedup ---

7653	        text = re.sub(

7662	        text = self._strip_phantom_citations(text)
