7677	        """Programmatic post-interpretation claim verification.
7678	
7679	        Enhanced for 8-phase pipeline with optional briefing for:
7680	        - Evidence coverage check (what % of clusters cited)
7681	        - Restatement detection (Jaccard overlap = parroting)
7682	        - Sub-question coverage
7683	
7684	        Appends a verification step to the notebook with results.
7685	        Returns a summary dict.
7686	        """
7687	        # Find the interpretation step
7688	        interp_step = None
7689	        for step in self._notebook.steps:
7690	            if step.tool_name == "interpret_results" and step.result.success:
7691	                interp_step = step
7692	                break
7693	
7694	        if not interp_step:
7695	            return {}
7696	
7697	        content = interp_step.result.markdown
7698	
7699	        # Category mismatch check (reuses existing method)
7700	        category_mismatches = self._verify_interpretation_claims(content)
7701	
7702	        # Numerical presence check
7703	        all_cites = re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', content)
7704	        pattern = re.compile(
7705	            r'([^.!?\n]{10,120})\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]'
7706	        )
7707	
7708	        total_checked = 0
7709	        verified = 0
7710	        for match in pattern.finditer(content):
7711	            claim_text = match.group(1).strip()
7712	            ev_id = match.group(2)
7713	
7714	            if ev_id not in self._evidence_store:
7715	                continue
7716	
7717	            nums_in_claim = re.findall(r'(\d+\.?\d*)', claim_text)
7718	            if not nums_in_claim:
7719	                continue
7720	
7721	            total_checked += 1
7722	            ev_stmt = self._evidence_store[ev_id].get(
7723	                "statement", "",
7724	            ).lower()
7725	            key_num = nums_in_claim[-1]  # Closest to citation
7726	
7727	            if key_num in ev_stmt:
7728	                verified += 1
7729	
7730	        report = {
7731	            "total_citations": len(all_cites),
7732	            "unique_citations": len(set(all_cites)),
7733	            "total_claims_checked": total_checked,
7734	            "verified": verified,
7735	            "mismatches": len(category_mismatches),
7736	            "mismatch_details": category_mismatches[:10],
7737	        }
7738	
7739	        # Enhanced metrics when briefing is available
7740	        if briefing:
7741	            # Evidence coverage: what % of clusters have citations
7742	            cited_eids = set(all_cites)
7743	            clusters = briefing.get("clusters", [])
7744	            learnings = briefing.get("learnings", [])
7745	            clusters_cited = 0
7746	            for cluster in clusters:
7747	                cluster_eids = set()
7748	                for idx in cluster.get("learning_indices", []):
7749	                    if idx < len(learnings):
7750	                        cluster_eids.update(
7751	                            learnings[idx].get("evidence_ids", []),
7752	                        )
7753	                if cluster_eids & cited_eids:
7754	                    clusters_cited += 1
7755	            report["cluster_coverage"] = round(
7756	                clusters_cited / max(len(clusters), 1), 3,
7757	            )
7758	
7759	            # Category coverage
7760	            all_categories = set(
7761	                l.get("category", "general") for l in learnings
7762	            )
7763	            cited_categories = set()
7764	            for eid in cited_eids:
7765	                ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
7766	                cited_categories.add(ev.get("fact_category", "general"))
7767	            report["category_coverage"] = round(
7768	                len(cited_categories & all_categories)
7769	                / max(len(all_categories), 1),
7770	                3,
7771	            )
7772	
7773	            # Restatement detection (parroting)
7774	            sentences = re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', content)
7775	            parroted = 0
7776	            for sent in sentences:
7777	                sent_words = set(
7778	                    w.lower() for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sent.lower())
7779	                )
7780	                if not sent_words:
7781	                    continue
7782	                for eid in self._evidence_ids[:200]:
7783	                    ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
7784	                    ev_stmt = ev.get("statement", "")
7785	                    ev_words = set(
7786	                        w.lower()
7787	                        for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ev_stmt.lower())
7788	                    )
7789	                    if not ev_words:
7790	                        continue
7791	                    jaccard = (
7792	                        len(sent_words & ev_words)
7793	                        / max(len(sent_words | ev_words), 1)
7794	                    )
7795	                    if jaccard > 0.5:
7796	                        parroted += 1
7797	                        break
7798	            report["parroting_ratio"] = round(
7799	                parroted / max(len(sentences), 1), 3,
7800	            )
7801	
7802	            # Sub-question coverage
7803	            sub_questions = briefing.get("sub_questions", [])
7804	            sq_covered = 0
7805	            for sq in sub_questions:
7806	                sq_words = set(
7807	                    w for w in sq.lower().split() if len(w) > 3
7808	                )
7809	                interp_lower = content.lower()
7810	                overlap = sum(1 for w in sq_words if w in interp_lower)
7811	                if overlap >= 2:
7812	                    sq_covered += 1
7813	            report["sub_question_coverage"] = round(
7814	                sq_covered / max(len(sub_questions), 1), 3,
7815	            )
7816	
7817	        # Build verification report markdown
7818	        report_lines = [
7819	            "**Claim Verification Report:**",
7820	            f"- Citations: {len(all_cites)} total, "
7821	            f"{len(set(all_cites))} unique",
7822	            f"- Numerical claims checked: {total_checked}",
7823	            f"- Numbers verified in source: {verified}/{total_checked}",
7824	            f"- Category mismatches: {len(category_mismatches)}",
7825	        ]
7826	        if briefing:
7827	            report_lines.extend([
7828	                f"- Cluster coverage: "
7829	                f"{report.get('cluster_coverage', 0):.0%}",
7830	                f"- Parroting ratio: "
7831	                f"{report.get('parroting_ratio', 0):.0%}",
7832	                f"- Sub-question coverage: "
7833	                f"{report.get('sub_question_coverage', 0):.0%}",
7834	            ])
7835	        if category_mismatches:
7836	            report_lines.append("\n**Category Mismatches:**")
7837	            for mm in category_mismatches[:5]:
7838	                report_lines.append(
7839	                    f"- {mm['ev_id']}: claim \"{mm['claim'][:50]}\" "
7840	                    f"({mm['claim_category']}) vs evidence "
7841	                    f"\"{mm['ev_statement'][:50]}\" ({mm['ev_category']})"
7842	                )
7843	
7844	        verify_step = AnalysisStep(
7845	            step_number=self._notebook.step_count + 1,
7846	            reasoning="Programmatic claim-evidence verification",
7847	            tool_name="verify_claims",
7848	            result=ToolResult(
7849	                success=True,
7850	                tool_name="verify_claims",
7851	                markdown="\n".join(report_lines),
7852	                source_evidence_ids=list(set(all_cites))[:20],
7853	                insights=[
7854	                    f"Verified {verified}/{total_checked} numerical "
7855	                    f"claims against source evidence",
7856	                ],
7857	                statistics=report,
7858	            ),
7859	            elapsed_seconds=0.0,
7860	        )
7861	        self._notebook.add_step(verify_step)
7862	
7863	        return report
7864

7866	    # ReAct loop helpers (legacy mode)
7867	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------

7869	    async def _decide(self, iteration: int) -> ReactDecision:
7870	        """Ask the LLM which tool to use next."""
7871	        available = self._registry.available_tools(self._notebook.has_data)
7872	        tool_descriptions = self._registry.get_tool_descriptions(
7873	            self._notebook.has_data,
7874	        )
7875	        notebook_summary = self._notebook.summary_for_llm()
7876	
7877	        evidence_count = len(self._evidence_ids)
7878	        has_structured = any(
7879	            self._evidence_store.get(eid, {}).get("structured_data")
7880	            for eid in self._evidence_ids[:50]
7881	        )
7882	
7883	        # Compact prompt — keep under 800 tokens to avoid Qwen timeouts
7884	        # (Qwen 3.5 Plus latency spikes on 2nd+ structured calls)
7885	        tools_short = ", ".join(available)
7886	        done_tools = ", ".join(
7887	            s.tool_name for s in self._notebook.steps if s.result.success
7888	        ) or "none"
7889	        dp_count = len(self._notebook.data_points)
7890	
7891	        prompt = (
7892	            f"Topic: {self._query[:120]}\n"
7893	            f"Evidence: {evidence_count} pieces | "
7894	            f"Data points: {dp_count} | "
7895	            f"Structured data: {has_structured}\n"
7896	            f"Done: {done_tools}\n"
7897	            f"Available: {tools_short}\n\n"
7898	            f"Rules:\n"
7899	            f"1. extract_numeric_data FIRST if data points = 0\n"
7900	            f"2. Don't repeat succeeded tools\n"
7901	            f"3. Need: stats + comparison/meta before stop\n"
7902	            f"4. Max {_MAX_ITERATIONS} steps\n\n"
7903	            f"Pick one tool or 'stop'. Give reasoning."
7904	        )
7905	
7906	        system = (
7907	            "Pick the next analysis tool. Respond with reasoning and action."
7908	        )
7909	
7910	        decision = await asyncio.wait_for(
7911	            self._client.generate_structured(
7912	                prompt=prompt,
7913	                schema=ReactDecision,
7914	                system=system,
7915	                max_tokens=512,
7916	                timeout=60,
7917	            ),
7918	            timeout=75,
7919	        )
7920	
7921	        # Validate the action is a known tool or "stop"
7922	        if decision.action != "stop" and decision.action not in available:
7923	            logger.warning(
7924	                "[react] LLM picked unavailable tool '%s', mapping to fallback",
7925	                decision.action,
7926	            )
7927	            if (
7928	                not self._notebook.has_data
7929	                and "extract_numeric_data" in available
7930	            ):
7931	                decision.action = "extract_numeric_data"
7932	                decision.reasoning = (
7933	                    f"Falling back to extract_numeric_data "
7934	                    f"('{decision.action}' unavailable)"
7935	                )
7936	            elif available:
7937	                decision.action = available[0]
7938	                decision.reasoning = f"Falling back to {available[0]}"
7939	            else:
7940	                decision.action = "stop"
7941	
7942	        return decision
7943	
7944	    async def _execute_tool(
7945	        self, iteration: int, decision: ReactDecision,
7946	    ) -> AnalysisStep:
7947	        """Execute a tool and return an AnalysisStep."""
7948	        tool_def = self._registry.get_tool(decision.action)
7949	        step_start = time.monotonic()
7950	
7951	        if not tool_def or not tool_def.execute:
7952	            return AnalysisStep(
7953	                step_number=iteration,
7954	                reasoning=decision.reasoning,
7955	                tool_name=decision.action,
7956	                result=ToolResult(
7957	                    success=False,
7958	                    tool_name=decision.action,
7959	                    markdown=f"Unknown tool: {decision.action}",
7960	                    error=f"Tool '{decision.action}' not found in registry",
7961	                ),
7962	                elapsed_seconds=0.0,
7963	            )
7964	
7965	        try:
7966	            result = await asyncio.wait_for(
7967	                tool_def.execute(
7968	                    evidence_store=self._evidence_store,
7969	                    data_points=self._notebook.data_points,
7970	                    client=self._client,
7971	                    **decision.action_input,
7972	                ),
7973	                timeout=_TOOL_TIMEOUT,
7974	            )
7975	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
7976	            result = ToolResult(
7977	                success=False,
7978	                tool_name=decision.action,
7979	                markdown=f"Tool timed out after {_TOOL_TIMEOUT}s",
7980	                error=f"Timeout after {_TOOL_TIMEOUT}s",
7981	            )
7982	        except Exception as exc:
7983	            result = ToolResult(
7984	                success=False,
7985	                tool_name=decision.action,
7986	                markdown=f"Tool execution error: {str(exc)[:200]}",
7987	                error=str(exc)[:500],
7988	            )
7989	
7990	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - step_start
7991	
7992	        return AnalysisStep(
7993	            step_number=iteration,
7994	            reasoning=decision.reasoning,
7995	            tool_name=decision.action,
7996	            result=result,
7997	            elapsed_seconds=round(elapsed, 3),
7998	        )

8000	    async def _run_fallback(self) -> None:
8001	        """Deterministic minimal analysis without LLM decisions."""
8002	        logger.info("[react] Running deterministic fallback analysis")
8003	
8004	        # Step 1: Extract data
8005	        await self._run_fallback_tool(
8006	            "extract_numeric_data",
8007	            "Fallback: extracting numeric data from evidence",
8008	        )

8025	    ) -> None:
8026	        """Execute a single tool as part of fallback analysis."""
8027	        tool = self._registry.get_tool(tool_name)
8028	        if not tool or not tool.execute:
8029	            return
8030	        try:
8031	            result = await asyncio.wait_for(
8032	                tool.execute(

8048	            logger.warning(
8049	                "[react] Fallback %s failed: %s", tool_name, str(exc)[:200],

8052	    async def _interpret_results(self) -> None:
8053	        """Use Qwen's reasoning to interpret raw tool outputs into insights.
8054	
8055	        This is the critical step that separates "regex + scipy" from
8056	        "analyst with reasoning." The LLM reads the raw extraction +
8057	        statistics and produces:
8058	        - Technology-level comparison (not URL-level)
8059	        - Key findings with specific numbers and [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens
8060	        - Cost-effectiveness ranking
8061	        - Insights the section writer can directly use
8062	
8063	        Uses generate() (prose mode) — NOT generate_structured() — because
8064	        we want rich markdown with inline citations, not constrained JSON.
8065	        """
8066	        # Build evidence ID → short label mapping for the prompt
8067	        ev_labels = {}
8068	        for step in self._notebook.steps:
8069	            if not step.result.success:
8070	                continue
8071	            for dp in step.result.data_points_produced:
8072	                eid = dp.get("evidence_id", "")
8073	                if eid and eid not in ev_labels:
8074	                    # Build a useful label from the evidence
8075	                    ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
8076	                    title = ev.get("source_title", "")[:60]
8077	                    ev_labels[eid] = title or eid[:16]
8078	
8079	        # Collect data points with FULL evidence context (not truncated labels)
8080	        # This prevents Qwen from misinterpreting "40% less expensive" as
8081	        # "40% removal" — the full statement provides the semantic context.
8082	        dp_lines = []
8083	        seen_ev = set()
8084	        for dp in self._notebook.data_points[:60]:
8085	            eid = dp.get("evidence_id", "")
8086	            value = dp.get("value", "")
8087	            unit = dp.get("unit", "")
8088	
8089	            # Pre-format large numbers for readability
8090	            try:
8091	                num = float(str(value).replace(",", ""))
8092	                if abs(num) >= 1e9:
8093	                    value = f"~${num / 1e9:.2f} billion" if unit == "USD" else f"~{num / 1e9:.2f}B"
8094	                elif abs(num) >= 1e6:
8095	                    value = f"~${num / 1e6:.1f} million" if unit == "USD" else f"~{num / 1e6:.1f}M"
8096	            except (ValueError, TypeError):
8097	                pass
8098	
8099	            # Include the FULL evidence statement (not truncated label)
8100	            # so Qwen can read "40% less expensive" not just "GAC was: 40%"
8101	            ev = self._evidence_store.get(eid, {})
8102	            stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:150]
8103	
8104	            if eid and eid not in seen_ev:
8105	                dp_lines.append(
8106	                    f"- {value} {unit} — \"{stmt}\" [{eid}]"
8107	                )
8108	                seen_ev.add(eid)
8109	            elif eid:
8110	                # Same evidence, different data point — just note the value
8111	                dp_lines.append(f"  also: {value} {unit} [{eid}]")
8112	
8113	        raw_data = "\n".join(dp_lines) if dp_lines else "No structured data."
8114	
8115	        # Collect raw stats
8116	        stats_text = ""
8117	        for step in self._notebook.steps:
8118	            if step.result.success and step.result.statistics:
8119	                stats_text += (
8120	                    f"\n{step.tool_name}: "
8121	                    f"{json.dumps(step.result.statistics, default=str)[:300]}"
8122	                )
8123	
8124	        prompt = (
8125	            f"You are a research analyst. Interpret the following raw data "
8126	            f"to answer the research question.\n\n"
8127	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION: {self._query}\n\n"
8128	            f"RAW EXTRACTED DATA ({len(self._notebook.data_points)} points):\n"
8129	            f"{raw_data}\n\n"
8130	            f"STATISTICS:\n{stats_text}\n\n"
8131	            f"INSTRUCTIONS:\n"
8132	            f"1. Group findings by TECHNOLOGY or METHOD (e.g., 'Reverse "
8133	            f"Osmosis', 'Granular Activated Carbon', 'Ion Exchange'), NOT "
8134	            f"by source URL\n"
8135	            f"2. For each technology, state: effectiveness (with numbers), "
8136	            f"cost (if available), limitations\n"
8137	            f"3. Rank technologies by effectiveness AND affordability\n"
8138	            f"4. For EVERY claim with a number, cite the source using "
8139	            f"[CITE:ev_xxx] format with the evidence ID from the data\n"
8140	            f"5. Identify what the data does NOT tell us (gaps)\n"
8141	            f"6. Be specific — never say 'several studies show' without "
8142	            f"numbers and citations\n"
8143	            f"7. READ THE QUOTED STATEMENT carefully before interpreting "
8144	            f"each number. '40% less expensive' is a COST metric, NOT a "
8145	            f"removal rate. '7.2% CAGR' is market growth, NOT removal\n"
8146	            f"8. Do NOT create false ranges from separate sources. If one "
8147	            f"study reports 2 kWh and another reports 313 kWh, those are "
8148	            f"TWO separate findings, not a '2-313 kWh range'\n"
8149	            f"9. Format large numbers readably: $2.09 billion NOT "
8150	            f"$2,089,500,000. Use B/M suffixes for billions/millions\n"
8151	            f"10. ONLY cite evidence IDs starting with 'ev_'. NEVER cite "
8152	            f"tool names\n\n"
8153	            f"Produce 300-600 words of curated analysis with inline "
8154	            f"citations. NO raw data dumps, NO tables — just analytical "
8155	            f"prose with specific numbers."
8156	        )
8157	
8158	        system = (
8159	            "You are a senior research analyst producing publication-quality "
8160	            "insights. Every claim must have a specific number and a "
8161	            "[CITE:ev_xxx] citation. Be concise, analytical, and critical."
8162	        )
8163	
8164	        interpret_timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT", "180"))
8165	        try:
8166	            response = await asyncio.wait_for(
8167	                self._client.generate(
8168	                    prompt=prompt,
8169	                    system=system,
8170	                    max_tokens=4096,
8171	                    temperature=0.3,
8172	                    timeout=interpret_timeout,
8173	                ),
8174	                timeout=interpret_timeout + 30,
8175	            )
8176	
8177	            content = response.content.strip()
8178	            if not content or len(content) < 100:
8179	                logger.warning(
8180	                    "[react] Interpretation produced too little content: "
8181	                    "%d chars", len(content),
8182	                )
8183	                return
8184	
8185	            # Validate: extract ALL [CITE:xxx] tokens and check they exist
8186	            all_cited = re.findall(r'\[CITE:([^\]]+)\]', content)
8187	            valid_ids = [
8188	                eid for eid in all_cited
8189	                if eid in self._evidence_store
8190	            ]
8191	            phantom_ids = [
8192	                eid for eid in all_cited
8193	                if eid not in self._evidence_store
8194	            ]
8195	
8196	            if phantom_ids:
8197	                logger.warning(
8198	                    "[react] Interpretation has %d phantom citations: %s",
8199	                    len(phantom_ids), phantom_ids[:5],
8200	                )
8201	                # Remove phantom citations from output
8202	                for pid in set(phantom_ids):
8203	                    content = content.replace(f"[CITE:{pid}]", "")
8204	
8205	            # Post-interpretation claim verification: check that numbers
8206	            # near each [CITE:ev_xxx] actually appear in that evidence.
8207	            # This catches "40% removal" when evidence says "40% cheaper".
8208	            mismatches = self._verify_interpretation_claims(content)
8209	            if mismatches:
8210	                logger.warning(
8211	                    "[react] Interpretation has %d claim mismatches",
8212	                    len(mismatches),
8213	                )
8214	                # Append warnings to the content so the section writer
8215	                # can see which claims may be inaccurate
8216	                warning_lines = ["\n\n**Verification Notes:**"]
8217	                for mm in mismatches[:5]:
8218	                    warning_lines.append(
8219	                        f"- Claim \"{mm['claim'][:60]}\" cites {mm['ev_id'][:20]} "
8220	                        f"which says: \"{mm['ev_statement'][:80]}\""
8221	                    )
8222	                content += "\n".join(warning_lines)
8223	
8224	            # Add as the final step in the notebook
8225	            step = AnalysisStep(
8226	                step_number=self._notebook.step_count + 1,
8227	                reasoning="LLM interpretation of raw analysis results",
8228	                tool_name="interpret_results",
8229	                result=ToolResult(
8230	                    success=True,
8231	                    tool_name="interpret_results",
8232	                    markdown=content,
8233	                    source_evidence_ids=list(set(valid_ids)),
8234	                    insights=[
8235	                        "LLM-synthesized analysis with per-claim citations",
8236	                    ],
8237	                ),
8238	                elapsed_seconds=0.0,
8239	            )
8240	            self._notebook.add_step(step)
8241	
8242	            logger.info(
8243	                "[react] Interpretation complete: %d chars, %d citations "
8244	                "(%d valid, %d phantom)",
8245	                len(content), len(all_cited), len(valid_ids),
8246	                len(phantom_ids),
8247	            )
8248	
8249	        except Exception as exc:
8250	            logger.warning(
8251	                "[react] Interpretation failed: %s: %s",
8252	                type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
8253	            )
8254	
8255	    def _verify_interpretation_claims(self, content: str) -> list[dict]:
8256	        """Lightweight post-interpretation verification.
8257	
8258	        For each [CITE:ev_xxx] in the output, extract the number closest
8259	        to it and check if that number appears in the cited evidence
8260	        statement. If not, flag as a potential misinterpretation.
8261	
8262	        This catches "GAC 40% removal" when evidence says "40% cheaper"
8263	        because the number 40 IS in the evidence but the surrounding
8264	        words don't match. For that we check if the claim sentence and
8265	        evidence share a key descriptor (removal/cost/efficiency/etc).
8266	        """
8267	        mismatches = []
8268	
8269	        # Find all citation contexts: text before [CITE:ev_xxx]
8270	        pattern = re.compile(
8271	            r'([^.!?\n]{10,120})\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]'
8272	        )
8273	
8274	        for match in pattern.finditer(content):
8275	            claim_text = match.group(1).strip()
8276	            ev_id = match.group(2)
8277	
8278	            if ev_id not in self._evidence_store:
8279	                continue
8280	
8281	            ev = self._evidence_store[ev_id]
8282	            ev_stmt = ev.get("statement", "").lower()
8283	
8284	            # Extract the key number from the claim (closest to the citation)
8285	            nums_in_claim = re.findall(r'(\d+\.?\d*)', claim_text)
8286	            if not nums_in_claim:
8287	                continue
8288	
8289	            # Check if the key number exists in the evidence
8290	            key_num = nums_in_claim[-1]  # Closest to the citation
8291	            if key_num not in ev_stmt:
8292	                # Number not in evidence — might be derived (e.g. 15x)
8293	                continue
8294	
8295	            # Number IS in evidence — now check semantic match
8296	            # Define category keywords
8297	            cost_words = {"cost", "price", "expensive", "affordable",
8298	                          "budget", "spending", "allocated", "funding",
8299	                          "billion", "million", "usd", "$"}
8300	            removal_words = {"removal", "removed", "efficiency", "reduction",
8301	                             "achieved", "treatment", "filtration", "adsorption"}
8302	            market_words = {"market", "share", "cagr", "growth", "valued",
8303	                            "projected", "revenue"}
8304	
8305	            claim_lower = claim_text.lower()
8306	            claim_is_cost = any(w in claim_lower for w in cost_words)
8307	            claim_is_removal = any(w in claim_lower for w in removal_words)
8308	            claim_is_market = any(w in claim_lower for w in market_words)
8309	
8310	            ev_is_cost = any(w in ev_stmt for w in cost_words)
8311	            ev_is_removal = any(w in ev_stmt for w in removal_words)
8312	            ev_is_market = any(w in ev_stmt for w in market_words)
8313	
8314	            # Flag if categories don't match (cost claim citing removal ev)
8315	            category_mismatch = False
8316	            if claim_is_removal and ev_is_cost and not ev_is_removal:
8317	                category_mismatch = True
8318	            if claim_is_cost and ev_is_removal and not ev_is_cost:
8319	                category_mismatch = True
8320	            if claim_is_removal and ev_is_market and not ev_is_removal:
8321	                category_mismatch = True
8322	
8323	            if category_mismatch:
8324	                mismatches.append({
8325	                    "claim": claim_text,
8326	                    "ev_id": ev_id,
8327	                    "ev_statement": ev.get("statement", ""),
8328	                    "claim_category": (
8329	                        "cost" if claim_is_cost else
8330	                        "removal" if claim_is_removal else
8331	                        "market" if claim_is_market else "other"
8332	                    ),
8333	                    "ev_category": (
8334	                        "cost" if ev_is_cost else
8335	                        "removal" if ev_is_removal else
8336	                        "market" if ev_is_market else "other"
8337	                    ),
8338	                })
8339	
8340	        return mismatches
8341	
8342	    def _is_sufficient(self) -> bool:
8343	        """Check if analysis has enough results to stop.
8344	
8345	        Requires at least 3 successful steps AND both statistics and
8346	        a comparison/meta tool. This prevents early stopping after
8347	        just extract + stats.
8348	        """
8349	        successful = [
8350	            s for s in self._notebook.steps if s.result.success
8351	        ]
8352	
8353	        if len(successful) < 3:
8354	            return False
8355	
8356	        has_stats = any(
8357	            s.tool_name in ("statistical_summary", "query_evidence_sql")
8358	            for s in successful
8359	        )
8360	        has_comparison = any(
8361	            s.tool_name in (
8362	                "comparison_table", "meta_analysis", "execute_python",
8363	                "rank_by_impact",
8364	            )
8365	            for s in successful
8366	        )
8367
